Reference to the flight leg for which this weight and balance loadsheet was prepared.

Foreign key linking to cargo.load_plan. Business justification: The W&B sheet is produced directly from the cargo load plan — loadmasters use the load plan as the source document for W&B calculations. Regulatory compliance and dispatch sign-off require traceabilit

Foreign key linking to crew.member. Business justification: Regulatory load sheet accountability: the loadmaster who prepares and signs the weight & balance sheet is a licensed crew member. Aviation regulators require traceability of load control sign-offs to
Tail number or registration code of the aircraft for which this weight and balance was calculated.
ICAO or IATA aircraft type designator (e.g., B738, A320, B77W) identifying the aircraft model.
Total weight of checked passenger baggage loaded in cargo holds.
Digital or physical signature of the pilot in command accepting the weight and balance loadsheet and authorizing departure.
Date and time when the pilot in command accepted and signed the weight and balance loadsheet.
Total weight of revenue cargo (freight and mail) loaded in cargo holds, excluding passenger baggage.

business_glossary_term: Centre of Gravity (CG) Aft Limit Percent Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC)
description: Aft limit of the certified CG envelope for this weight and configuration. CG must not be behind this limit.

business_glossary_term: Centre of Gravity (CG) Forward Limit Percent Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC)
description: Forward limit of the certified CG envelope for this weight and configuration. CG must not be ahead of this limit.

business_glossary_term: Centre of Gravity (CG) at Landing Percent Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC)
description: Estimated longitudinal centre of gravity position at landing expressed as percentage of mean aerodynamic chord. Must remain within certified CG envelope.

business_glossary_term: Centre of Gravity (CG) at Takeoff Percent Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC)
description: Longitudinal centre of gravity position at takeoff expressed as percentage of mean aerodynamic chord. Must be within certified CG envelope.
FAA or EASA dispatcher license number of the dispatcher who signed off on the loadsheet.
Name of the flight dispatcher who reviewed and signed off on the weight and balance loadsheet.
The weight of the aircraft including crew, crew baggage, catering, and all operational equipment but excluding fuel, passengers, cargo, and baggage. Also known as basic operating weight.
Date and time when the loadsheet was finalized and signed off, authorizing the flight for departure.

business_glossary_term: Aft Cargo Hold Weight in Kilograms
description: Total weight loaded in the aft cargo hold compartment.

business_glossary_term: Bulk Cargo Hold Weight in Kilograms
description: Total weight loaded in the bulk cargo hold compartment, typically used for loose baggage and small cargo items.

business_glossary_term: Forward Cargo Hold Weight in Kilograms
description: Total weight loaded in the forward cargo hold compartment.
Estimated weight of the aircraft at landing. Calculated as takeoff weight minus trip fuel. Must not exceed maximum landing weight.
Flag indicating whether the load distribution meets all structural and CG envelope requirements for safe flight.
Unique externally-known identifier for the weight and balance loadsheet document, typically assigned by the load control system or dispatcher.
Current lifecycle status of the weight and balance loadsheet. Final status indicates dispatcher sign-off and regulatory compliance approval.
Version number of the loadsheet. Incremented when corrections or updates are made prior to departure.
Weight of mail cargo loaded in cargo holds.
Maximum permissible landing weight for this aircraft as specified in the aircraft flight manual. Structural limit.
Maximum permissible takeoff weight for this aircraft under current conditions (runway length, temperature, altitude, obstacles). May be limited below certified MTOW.

business_glossary_term: Maximum Zero Fuel Weight (MZFW) in Kilograms
description: Maximum permissible zero fuel weight for this aircraft as specified in the aircraft flight manual. Structural limit.
Number of passengers seated in business class cabin.
Number of passengers seated in economy cabin.
Number of passengers seated in first class cabin.
Number of passengers seated in premium economy cabin.
Total number of passengers on board across all cabin classes (first, business, premium economy, economy).
Total weight of all passengers calculated using standard or actual passenger weights per regulatory requirements.
Date and time when the weight and balance loadsheet was initially prepared by the loadmaster or dispatcher.
System timestamp when this weight and balance record was first created in the database.
System timestamp when this weight and balance record was last modified in the database.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or clarifications related to the weight and balance calculation.
Flag indicating whether the flight carries special loads requiring additional handling or documentation (dangerous goods, live animals, human remains, etc.).
Total usable fuel on board at takeoff, including trip fuel, contingency, alternate, final reserve, and additional fuel.
Total weight of the aircraft at brake release for takeoff. Calculated as zero fuel weight plus takeoff fuel. Must not exceed maximum takeoff weight.
Total revenue payload weight including passengers, baggage, cargo, and mail.
Calculated horizontal stabilizer trim setting required for takeoff based on CG position and weight. Entered into flight management system or set manually.
Fuel required to fly from departure to destination airport under expected conditions.

business_glossary_term: Underfloor Cargo Unit Load Device (ULD) Count
description: Number of unit load devices (containers and pallets) loaded in underfloor cargo holds.
Total weight of the aircraft excluding usable fuel. Calculated as dry operating weight plus payload (passengers, baggage, cargo). Critical structural limit.
Unique surrogate identifier for the cabin class record within the airline's inventory system. Primary key for this reference master entity.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Cabin Class Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each cabin class has distinct cost structures (service, catering, crew ratios). Airlines track cabin-level profitability by allocating costs to cabin class cost centers for yield management and produc

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Airlines maintain separate GL revenue accounts by cabin class (first, business, premium economy, economy) for P&L reporting and RASK analysis. Revenue accounting systems post cabin-level revenue to di

Flight range category for which this cabin class configuration is applicable: 'short_haul' (under 3 hours), 'medium_haul' (3–6 hours), 'long_haul' (6–12 hours), 'ultra_long_haul' (over 12 hours), or 'all'. Drives route-specific product deployment and inventory segmentation.
reference: IATA Network Planning Standards; Jeppesen Flight Planning System

description: Standard checked baggage allowance in kilograms included with the base fare for this cabin class. Drives ancillary revenue calculations, passenger service standards, and GDS fare display. Governed by IATA Resolution 302 weight concept.
reference: IATA Resolution 302 — Baggage Allowance; DOT Baggage Disclosure Requirements

description: Number of checked baggage pieces included in the standard allowance for this cabin class under the piece concept. Applicable for routes governed by IATA piece concept (primarily transatlantic and transpacific). Complements baggage_allowance_kg for weight-concept routes.
reference: IATA Resolution 302 — Baggage Piece Concept; DOT Baggage Disclosure Requirements

description: Current lifecycle status of the cabin class record. 'active' indicates the cabin is currently offered and sellable; 'inactive' indicates temporarily suspended; 'retired' indicates permanently discontinued; 'pending' indicates configured but not yet activated for sale.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Cabin Class Lifecycle Management

description: Airline-internal alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cabin class within the carrier's Passenger Service System (PSS). Used in Amadeus Altéa Inventory for cabin-level availability and inventory management. May differ from the IATA designator when the airline operates sub-cabin variants.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Cabin Configuration; IATA SSIM Chapter 2

description: Commercial marketing name of the cabin class as presented to passengers and travel agents (e.g., 'First Class', 'Business Class', 'Premium Economy', 'Economy'). Used in passenger-facing communications, booking flows, and GDS displays.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; Amadeus Altéa PSS — Cabin Display Configuration

description: Standardized classification of the cabin into one of the four IATA-recognized commercial cabin tiers: first, business, premium_economy, or economy. Drives revenue management segmentation, yield optimization in Sabre AirVision, and inventory bucket hierarchy.
reference: IATA Resolution 728 — Cabin Class Hierarchy; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Maximum weight in kilograms permitted for carry-on baggage in the cabin for this cabin class. Used in ground handling operations, security screening coordination with TSA/EASA, and passenger service standards.
reference: IATA Resolution 302; TSA Carry-On Baggage Standards; EASA Cabin Safety Requirements

description: Indicates whether this cabin class can be sold on codeshare flights operated by partner airlines. When true, inventory allocations for this cabin are made available to codeshare partners through interline agreements. Drives GDS availability display for codeshare itineraries.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare Agreements; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Codeshare Inventory

description: Timestamp when this cabin class record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management — Record Control; ICAO Annex 6 Record Keeping

description: Date on which this cabin class definition is retired and no longer available for new bookings. Null for currently active cabin classes. Supports historical inventory analysis and regulatory record retention requirements.
reference: IATA SSIM; DOT Record Retention Requirements; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Date from which this cabin class definition becomes commercially active and available for sale. Used for seasonal product changes, new aircraft type introductions, and cabin reconfiguration projects. Aligns with IATA Season scheduling conventions.
reference: IATA SSIM — Schedule Season Dates; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Effective Date Management

description: Standard alphabetic prefix used in fare basis codes for this cabin class (e.g., 'Y' for Economy, 'C' for Business, 'F' for First). Aligns fare construction with IATA fare basis code conventions and drives revenue management fare class bucket assignment in Sabre AirVision.
reference: IATA Resolution 728 — Fare Basis Codes; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

business_glossary_term: Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) Mileage Accrual Multiplier
description: Multiplier applied to base miles flown to calculate Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) miles or points accrued for travel in this cabin class. For example, a value of 2.0 means passengers earn double miles. Feeds Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP accrual engine.
reference: IATA Resolution 787 — Frequent Flyer Programs; Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP Configuration

description: Code used to represent this cabin class in Global Distribution System (GDS) availability displays (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport). May differ from the internal cabin_code for legacy GDS compatibility. Ensures correct cabin identification in travel agent booking workflows.
reference: IATA PADIS Standards; Amadeus GDS Interface Specifications; Sabre Interface Standards

business_glossary_term: International Air Transport Association (IATA) Cabin Designator Code
description: Single-letter IATA standard cabin designator code identifying the commercial cabin class (e.g., F = First, C = Business, W = Premium Economy, Y = Economy). Used as the industry-standard booking class grouping code across Global Distribution Systems (GDS) and interline agreements.
reference: IATA Resolution 728 — Cabin Class Designators; IATA Passenger Services Conference Resolutions Manual

description: Type of In-Flight Entertainment (IFE) system available in this cabin class: 'seatback_screen' = personal seatback display, 'overhead_screen' = shared overhead monitors, 'personal_device' = bring-your-own-device streaming, 'none' = no IFE. Used in product definition and passenger experience standards.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; FAA Advisory Circular AC 25.1309

description: Indicates whether the seats in this cabin class convert to a fully flat sleeping surface (180-degree recline). True for premium long-haul First and Business class products. Critical for product differentiation, revenue management premium pricing, and GDS display.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Cabin Product Attributes

description: Level of airport lounge access included with this cabin class: 'none' = no lounge access, 'home_carrier' = access to the airline's own lounges only, 'partner' = access to partner airline lounges, 'all_alliance' = full alliance lounge network access. Drives ground handling instructions and passenger service delivery.
reference: IATA Interline Agreements; Amadeus Altéa PSS — Service Entitlement Configuration

description: Maximum percentage above physical seat capacity that bookings may be accepted for this cabin class under the airline's overbooking policy. For example, 10.00 means up to 110% of seats may be sold. Null when overbooking_allowed is false. Governed by DOT regulations.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250 — Oversales; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Overbooking Controls

description: Standard meal service type provided in this cabin class: 'full_meal' = multi-course meal service, 'light_meal' = single-course meal, 'snack' = snack and beverage only, 'buy_on_board' = no complimentary meal, 'none' = no food service. Drives catering uplift planning and galley configuration.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; IATA Airport Handling Manual (AHM) — Catering

description: Indicates whether overbooking is permitted for this cabin class. When true, the inventory system may accept bookings beyond physical seat capacity based on no-show forecasting models. Governed by DOT overbooking regulations and airline revenue management policy.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250 — Oversales; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Overbooking Controls

description: Type of in-seat power outlet available in this cabin class: 'ac_110v', 'ac_220v', 'usb_a', 'usb_c', or 'none'. Used in product specification, passenger communication, and aircraft configuration management in AMOS.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; FAA Advisory Circular AC 25.1353

description: Indicates whether passengers in this cabin class receive priority boarding privileges ahead of general boarding groups. Drives boarding sequence configuration in SITA Airport Management Platform and ground handling SOPs.
reference: IATA Airport Handling Manual (AHM); SITA Airport Management Platform

description: Indicates whether passengers in this cabin class are entitled to dedicated priority check-in counters or lanes. Drives airport operations configuration in SITA Airport Management Platform and ground handling service level agreements.
reference: IATA Airport Handling Manual (AHM); SITA Airport Management Platform

description: Numeric rank defining the commercial hierarchy of this cabin class relative to others (1 = highest/First, 4 = lowest/Economy). Used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management for yield optimization, upgrade logic, and inventory nesting rules. Lower number = higher revenue class.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Cabin Hierarchy; IATA Revenue Management Standards

description: Standard seat layout configuration code for this cabin class expressed as a row-abreast pattern (e.g., '2-2-2' for Business, '3-3-3' for Economy). Reflects the physical seat arrangement across the aircraft cross-section. Sourced from aircraft type configuration in AMOS.
reference: IATA SSIM Annex B — Aircraft Seat Configuration; AMOS Aircraft Configuration

description: Distance in inches between a point on one seat and the same point on the seat directly in front, representing legroom standard for this cabin class. Key product differentiation metric used in marketing, regulatory DOT reporting, and passenger experience benchmarking.
reference: FAA Advisory Circular AC 25.785; DOT Consumer Reporting Requirements; IATA Cabin Product Standards

description: Maximum recline distance in inches for seats in this cabin class. Distinguishes flat-bed (Business/First) from recliner (Premium Economy) and standard recline (Economy) product tiers. Used in product definition and passenger experience standards.
Width of the seat in inches for this cabin class, measured at the widest point of the seat cushion. Used in product specification, passenger comfort benchmarking, and marketing collateral.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; FAA Advisory Circular AC 25.785

description: Narrative description of the service standards, amenities, and passenger experience associated with this cabin class. Includes details such as meal service type, lounge access entitlement, priority boarding, and onboard entertainment offerings. Used for product definition and customer communication.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; Amadeus Altéa PSS — Service Definition

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this cabin class definition originates (e.g., 'ALTEA_INV' for Amadeus Altéa Inventory, 'SABRE_RM' for Sabre AirVision). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; ISO 9001 Data Governance

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cabin class record. Used for change tracking, data synchronization with downstream systems (GDS, Revenue Management), and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management — Record Control; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Change Management

description: Indicates whether passengers booked in this cabin class are eligible to receive complimentary or paid upgrades to a higher cabin class. Drives upgrade logic in Amadeus Altéa Inventory and Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP upgrade award processing.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Upgrade Rules; Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP Management

description: Indicates whether in-flight Wi-Fi connectivity is available in this cabin class. Drives ancillary service offerings, passenger communication, and product marketing. Complements IFE type for digital connectivity product definition.
reference: IATA Cabin Product Standards; FCC Airborne Mobile Services Regulations

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the fare class bucket record in the Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — RBD Bucket Entity

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: fare_class_bucket defines RBD buckets within a cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK establishes the cabin hierarchy for fare class buckets. cabin_code is red

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare. Business justification: Each fare class bucket controls availability for specific filed fares at that RBD level. fare_class_bucket has fare_basis_code (plain) but no fare_id FK. RM yield analysis and revenue dilution reporti

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Airline revenue accounting (IFRS 15) requires each fare class bucket to map to a specific GL revenue account for deferred/recognized revenue posting. Revenue management and finance reconciliation repo
Reference to the flight inventory leg record to which this fare class bucket belongs. Links the bucket to a specific flight-date-leg combination managed in Altéa Inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Flight Inventory Leg

description: Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: RM analysts configure fare class buckets at the route level; competitive intensity and profitability tier on the route drive bucket nesting and protection levels. Aviation RM systems require route-lev

Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: Fare class bucket configurations are season-specific; RM systems open new bucket sets each IATA season. Season-level bucket reporting and historical yield analysis require this FK to identify which se

Minimum number of days before departure that a ticket must be purchased to qualify for fares in this booking class (e.g., 7, 14, 21). A value of 0 indicates no advance purchase requirement. Governs ticketing time limit enforcement in Altéa.

Classification of the inventory allocation type for this bucket: own (operating carrier direct sale), codeshare (partner airline marketing), interline (interline ticketing agreement), group (group booking block), charter (charter operator block), blocked (operationally blocked seats). Drives revenue accounting and settlement routing.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Allotment Management

description: Authorized capacity (AU) — the maximum number of seats the revenue management system has authorized for sale in this booking class bucket for the flight leg. Set by Sabre AirVision yield optimization and loaded into Altéa Inventory. Core inventory control parameter.
reference: IATA Revenue Management Guidelines — Authorized Units (AU)

description: IATA standard availability status code transmitted to GDS systems (e.g., A = available, L = waitlist, R = request, C = closed). Distinct from bucket_status in that this is the externally-broadcast GDS code per IATA PADIS standards, while bucket_status is the internal operational state.
reference: IATA PADIS — Passenger and Airport Data Interchange Standards

description: Timestamp at which the current availability figures (seats_available, current_bookings, load_factor_pct) were captured from Altéa Inventory. Critical for GDS cache freshness validation and real-time availability SLA monitoring.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Snapshot; IATA NDC Standard

description: Booking limit (BL) — the maximum cumulative number of bookings permitted across this bucket and all lower-yield buckets nested beneath it. Enforced in real time by Altéa Inventory to prevent overselling beyond the authorized level.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Limit Control

description: Ordinal position of this RBD bucket within its cabin hierarchy, from highest-yield (rank 1) to lowest-yield. Used by Sabre AirVision and Altéa Inventory to determine availability cascade — a lower-ranked (higher-yield) bucket must be open before a higher-ranked (lower-yield) bucket can sell.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Bucket Nesting / IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Current availability status of the fare class bucket as broadcast to GDS and direct channels: open (seats available for sale), closed (no availability — bucket is shut by RM or capacity), waitlist (no confirmed seats but waitlist accepted), request_only (requires manual approval), suspended (temporarily removed from sale by operations).
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Status / IATA ATA Spec 2000

description: Monetary penalty charged to the passenger for a voluntary itinerary change on a ticket issued under this booking class, expressed in the filing currency. A value of 0.00 indicates no change fee. Commercially sensitive pricing parameter.
reference: ATPCO Category 31 — Voluntary Changes; IATA Resolution 024

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the change fee amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency fare filing and BSP/ARC settlement.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; IATA BSP Settlement

description: Indicates whether this fare class bucket is allocated for codeshare inventory (True) — i.e., seats sold under a partner airline's flight number — or is for the operating carrier's own inventory (False). Governs interline proration and codeshare revenue accounting.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Codeshare Allocation

description: IATA two-letter airline designator of the codeshare marketing partner for whom this bucket allocation is reserved (e.g., LH, BA, AA). Null for own-carrier inventory. Used for interline settlement and codeshare capacity reporting.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare; IATA Airline Designator Registry

description: Number of confirmed bookings currently held against this fare class bucket. Updated in near-real-time from Altéa Inventory as PNRs are created, modified, or cancelled. Used to compute remaining availability.
Calendar date from which this fare class bucket configuration becomes active for sale. Used for seasonal inventory planning and schedule-period bucket management in Altéa Inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Inventory Period Management; IATA SSIM

description: Calendar date after which this fare class bucket configuration is no longer active. Null indicates the bucket remains open-ended until explicitly closed. Supports seasonal and schedule-period inventory lifecycle management.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Inventory Period Management; IATA SSIM

description: IATA fare basis code associated with the primary fare filed under this booking class bucket (e.g., YOWUS, BAPUS21). Links the inventory bucket to the fare tariff filed with ATPCO. Used for ticketing, interline proration, and BSP/ARC settlement.
reference: IATA Resolution 024 — Fare Basis Code; ATPCO Fare Filing Standards

description: Branded fare family or product bundle code associated with this booking class (e.g., BASIC, STANDARD, FLEX, BUSINESS_PLUS). Aligns the RBD bucket with the airline's branded fare product hierarchy for ancillary bundling and NDC offer construction.
reference: IATA NDC Standard — Offer and Order Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Number of seats within this bucket reserved as a group block allocation. A value of 0 indicates no group block. Group blocks are managed separately from individual seat availability and are subject to group contract terms.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Inventory Management; IATA Group Travel Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the itinerary booked under this fare class may be voluntarily changed (True) or is a fixed non-changeable ticket (False). Relevant for IROP handling, voluntary rebooking, and ancillary change-fee revenue.
reference: ATPCO Category 31 — Voluntary Changes; DOT 14 CFR Part 259

description: Indicates whether tickets issued under this booking class are eligible for a full or partial refund (True) or are non-refundable (False). Drives customer service handling, involuntary rerouting compensation, and DOT consumer protection compliance.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 259 — Consumer Protections; ATPCO Category 33 — Voluntary Refunds

description: Timestamp of the most recent authorized unit or booking limit update pushed from Sabre AirVision to Altéa Inventory. Enables audit of RM optimization cycle frequency and latency monitoring between the two systems.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Optimization Cycle; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Current load factor for this fare class bucket expressed as a percentage (current_bookings / authorized_units × 100). Stored as a snapshot field for real-time RM monitoring and GDS availability optimization. Aligns with IATA load factor (percentage of seats filled) definition.
reference: IATA Standard Schedules Information Manual — Load Factor Definition

description: Maximum number of days the passenger may remain at the destination before return travel must commence for fares in this booking class. A null value indicates no maximum stay restriction. Standard fare restriction filed with ATPCO.

Minimum number of days the passenger must remain at the destination before return travel is permitted for fares in this booking class. A value of 0 indicates no minimum stay restriction. Standard fare restriction filed with ATPCO.

Fractional multiplier applied to physical seat capacity to derive the authorized overbooking level for this bucket (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% overbooking). Set by revenue management based on historical no-show and cancellation rates. Commercially sensitive — drives denied boarding exposure. Governed by DOT 14 CFR Part 250.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250 — Oversales; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Overbooking Control

description: Number of seats protected (reserved) for higher-yield demand in this bucket, as computed by the EMSR (Expected Marginal Seat Revenue) optimization in Sabre AirVision. Prevents lower-yield bookings from displacing higher-yield passengers. Core RM optimization output.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — EMSR Optimization; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Single uppercase IATA letter identifying the booking class (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V, H, K, L, S, N, O, G). The RBD is the atomic unit of inventory control in Altéa Inventory and the direct input to Sabre AirVision yield optimization. Governed by IATA Resolution 728.

Timestamp when this fare class bucket record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SLA compliance tracking per enterprise data governance standards.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance — Audit Trail; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this fare class bucket record in the Silver layer. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit compliance.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance — Audit Trail; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Current number of seats available for sale in this booking class bucket at the time of the last inventory snapshot. Derived operationally as booking_limit minus current_bookings, but stored as a first-class field for GDS availability response performance. Consumed by GDS channels and direct booking engines.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Count / IATA NDC Standard

description: Identifies the operational system of record that originated or last updated this fare class bucket record: ALTEA (Amadeus Altéa Inventory), SABRE_AV (Sabre AirVision Revenue Management), MANUAL (manually entered override), MIGRATION (data migration load). Supports data lineage and audit in the Silver layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance — Data Lineage Standards

description: Current number of waitlisted booking requests queued against this fare class bucket. When seats become available (e.g., cancellations), Altéa Inventory automatically clears waitlisted PNRs in queue order.

Revenue management yield classification assigned to this bucket by Sabre AirVision, grouping RBDs into broad yield tiers for reporting and optimization. Commercially sensitive as it reveals pricing strategy segmentation.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Yield Class Segmentation

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the flight inventory record representing the sellable seat inventory for a specific flight-date combination in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system.

Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft. Business justification: Flight inventory is opened on a specific tail number for each departure. Operations Control Center assigns aircraft to flights; inventory management needs tail number for MEL item applicability, actua

Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: When equipment changes occur (equipment_change_indicator on flight_inventory), the inventory system must reference the active cabin configuration to recompute class capacities (business_class_capacity

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Flight operations are cost centers in airline accounting. CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) calculations require linking flight inventory to cost centers for profitability analysis and manageme

Foreign key linking to route.route_fleet_assignment. Business justification: Fleet assignment determines aircraft type, seat configuration, and cabin class capacity that inventory must control. Revenue management bucket authorization, overbooking limits, and codeshare allocati

Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Flight inventory controls real-time seat availability for specific flight numbers defined in route planning. Revenue management, codeshare allocation, and GDS distribution all require linking daily in

Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Flight inventory capacity records must link to origin station for slot-coordinated market compliance reporting, station-level load factor dashboards, and hub capacity planning. Aviation revenue manage
FK to route.route.route_id — Flight inventory must reference the route for network-level availability queries, codeshare allocation management, and seasonal capacity planning.
Reference to the scheduled flight leg for which this inventory record is maintained. Links to the flight master entity in the flight operations domain.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Foreign key linking to route.seasonal_schedule. Business justification: Flight inventory operationalizes a specific seasonal schedule template. Linking inventory to seasonal_schedule enables variance analysis (planned vs actual capacity), schedule change impact assessment

Native primary key or reference identifier assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system to this flight inventory record. Used for system-of-record traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational PSS.

Revenue management-approved seat count authorized for sale on this flight-date, which may differ from total physical capacity due to operational holds, crew seat requirements, or regulatory restrictions. This is the effective inventory ceiling used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot broadcast to GDS and direct booking channels. Reflects the point-in-time at which seats_available and fare class bucket statuses were last synchronized with Amadeus Altéa.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787; IATA NDC Standard

description: Number of days prior to departure at which this inventory snapshot was captured, representing the position on the booking curve (e.g., 90, 60, 30, 14, 7, 1). Used by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management for demand forecasting and fare class optimization.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Total number of seats allocated to the Business Class cabin on this flight-date. Reflects the aircraft configuration and any cabin reconfiguration applied for this specific flight.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Code indicating any channel-specific sales restrictions applied to this flight inventory (e.g., 'DIRECT_ONLY' for web/app exclusive fares, 'GDS_BLOCKED' for GDS channel closure, 'NDC_ONLY' for IATA NDC channel exclusivity). Controls distribution channel access to inventory.
reference: IATA NDC (New Distribution Capability) Standard; IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Flag indicating whether this flight inventory record includes codeshare seat allocations from partner airlines. When true, a portion of the authorized capacity is reserved for codeshare partner bookings under interline ticketing agreements.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 (Codeshare); Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Number of seats within the authorized capacity ring-fenced for codeshare partner airline bookings on this flight-date. Managed under bilateral codeshare agreements and tracked separately from own-metal inventory for revenue accounting and BSP settlement.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 (Codeshare); IATA BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan); Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Timestamp when this flight inventory record was first created in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system, typically at the time of schedule publication or inventory initialization for the flight-date.
Scheduled local departure date of the flight leg at the origin station. Together with flight_number, forms the primary business key for inventory lookup by GDS and booking channels.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival station of this flight leg (e.g., LAX, CDG). Used to identify the inventory leg destination in route network and GDS availability queries.
reference: IATA Airport Codes Standard; IATA Resolution 787

description: Total number of seats allocated to the Economy Class cabin on this flight-date. Typically the largest cabin and primary driver of load factor and revenue management decisions.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Flag indicating whether the aircraft type assigned to this flight-date has been changed from the originally scheduled equipment. Equipment changes may alter cabin configuration, total capacity, and fare class bucket allocations requiring inventory rebalancing.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops

description: Serialized representation of the active fare class bucket (booking class) configuration for this flight-date, listing each IATA booking class code and its authorized seat allocation (e.g., 'Y:20,B:15,M:10,Q:5'). Managed by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management and consumed by Amadeus Altéa for availability control.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Total number of seats allocated to the First Class cabin on this flight-date. Reflects the aircraft configuration and any cabin reconfiguration applied for this specific flight.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: IATA-format airline designator and numeric flight number (e.g., AA101) identifying the marketed flight for which inventory is being managed. Used by GDS and direct channels to query availability.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; IATA Airline Designator Standard

description: Single-letter availability status code broadcast to Global Distribution Systems (GDS) representing the highest open booking class on this flight-date (e.g., 'Y' for full availability, 'L' for restricted availability). Conforms to IATA availability message standards.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; IATA PADIS (Passenger and Airport Data Interchange Standards); GDS Availability Standards

description: Number of seats currently held under group booking reservations (typically 10+ passengers under a single PNR group) on this flight-date. Group holds reduce available inventory for individual bookings and are subject to group ticketing deadlines.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Number of seats allocated for interline ticketing partner bookings on this flight-date under IATA interline agreements. Distinct from codeshare allocations and tracked for ARC/BSP proration and revenue accounting.
reference: IATA Resolution 780 (Interline); IATA ARC (Airlines Reporting Corporation); Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Indicates whether inventory management for this flight-date is operating under MANUAL (revenue analyst override), AUTOMATED (Sabre AirVision algorithmic control), or HYBRID (automated with manual overrides) mode. Affects how fare class bucket changes are applied.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Current operational status of the flight inventory record in Amadeus Altéa. OPEN indicates seats are available for sale; CLOSED indicates no further sales permitted; WAITLIST indicates demand exceeds supply and waitlist is active; SUSPENDED indicates temporary hold on sales; CANCELLED indicates the flight has been cancelled and inventory is frozen.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Date on which the last available seat on this flight-date was sold or the inventory was closed. Used for post-departure revenue analysis and booking curve benchmarking.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this flight inventory record, including any changes to seat counts, fare class buckets, overbooking parameters, or inventory status. Critical for real-time availability synchronization with GDS.

Current load factor expressed as a percentage, calculated as seats_sold divided by authorized_capacity multiplied by 100. A key revenue management KPI representing the proportion of available seat capacity that has been sold. Used by Sabre AirVision for yield optimization decisions.
reference: IATA WATS (World Air Transport Statistics); Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Resolution 787

description: Number of seats withheld from sale for operational purposes including deadhead crew positioning, airline staff travel, and aircraft ferry requirements. These seats are excluded from the sellable inventory pool.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; AIMS Crew Management System

description: Revenue management-approved overbooking multiplier expressed as a percentage above physical capacity (e.g., 105.00 means 5% overbooking allowed). Set by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management based on historical no-show and cancellation rates to maximize load factor while managing denied boarding risk.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT 14 CFR Part 250 (Oversales); IATA Resolution 787

description: Absolute maximum number of bookings permitted on this flight-date including the overbooking allowance. Computed from authorized_capacity multiplied by overbooking_factor and used as the hard ceiling in the Amadeus Altéa booking engine.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT 14 CFR Part 250; Amadeus Altéa Inventory System

description: Total number of seats allocated to the Premium Economy cabin on this flight-date. Nullable for aircraft configurations that do not include a Premium Economy cabin.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Revenue management classification assigned by Sabre AirVision to this flight-date based on demand forecast, booking curve position, and competitive pricing environment. Drives automated fare class bucket opening and closing decisions for yield optimization.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA RASK/CASK Framework

description: Flag indicating whether this flight-date has undergone a schedule change (time, equipment, or routing modification) since the original inventory record was created. Schedule changes may trigger IROP (Irregular Operations) rebooking workflows and inventory reallocation.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; DOT 14 CFR Part 259 (IROP); IATA Resolution 735

description: Current number of seats available for sale across all cabins on this flight-date, calculated as authorized_capacity minus seats_sold minus overbooking_buffer. This is the real-time availability figure broadcast to GDS and direct booking channels.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Total number of confirmed seat bookings (Pax) across all cabins and fare classes on this flight-date. Updated in real-time as reservations are created, modified, or cancelled in the Amadeus Altéa Reservation system.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Total number of physical seats available on the aircraft for this flight-date combination across all cabins. Derived from the aircraft configuration assigned to the flight and represents the maximum sellable inventory ceiling.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Current number of passengers on the waitlist for this flight-date when inventory_status is WAITLIST or when specific fare classes are closed. Waitlisted passengers are cleared in priority order as cancellations occur.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory System; IATA Resolution 787

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the inventory leg record within the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Leg Inventory Object

description: Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Revenue management systems configure fare class bucket authorization limits based on aircraft type's physical capacity, cabin configuration, and range. Inventory leg planning requires structured refer

Foreign key linking to route.block_time_standard. Business justification: Block time variance analysis and crew duty time compliance require linking each inventory leg to its applicable block time standard. Aviation operations teams use this to flag legs where scheduled_blo

Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: Inventory leg capacity per cabin class (authorized_capacity, total_seat_count) is authoritatively determined by the cabin configuration. Revenue management and seat inventory control require this link

Foreign key linking to airport.terminal_facility. Business justification: Terminal facility planners use inventory leg booked counts to forecast check-in counter demand, lounge occupancy, and gate capacity per terminal. Linking departure terminal normalizes the denormalized
Reference to the parent flight inventory record that owns this leg. Supports multi-leg itinerary nesting and through-availability calculations within the inventory domain.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Flight Inventory Object

description: Foreign key linking to flight.flight_leg. Business justification: Revenue management and load factor reporting require joining inventory_leg to the operated flight_leg for real-time status, delay impact, and OOOI events. Seat count reconciliation and IROP inventory

Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Schedule-inventory reconciliation and GDS availability broadcasting require inventory legs to be linked to their authoritative flight number record. The plain flight_number string on inventory_leg is

Foreign key linking to maintenance.release. Business justification: Regulatory requirement (EASA Part-M / FAA 14 CFR 43): no flight leg may depart without a valid Certificate of Release to Service. Linking inventory_leg to maintenance.release enables dispatch systems

Foreign key linking to route.carrier. Business justification: Interline proration, wet-lease inventory attribution, and carrier-level capacity reporting require a normalized FK from inventory leg to the operating carrier entity. operating_carrier_code is a denor

Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Revenue management inventory open/close decisions depend on origin station's slot-coordination status, hub classification, and operational hours. Aviation inventory planners routinely query station at

Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Route-level inventory reporting, slot compliance, and network capacity planning all require knowing which route each inventory leg belongs to. Aviation RM analysts expect inventory legs to be directly

Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: Inventory legs are opened and closed per IATA schedule season. Season-level inventory reporting, slot usage compliance (use-it-or-lose-it), and RM calibration require a direct FK. schedule_period_code
Terminal identifier at the destination airport where this leg arrives (e.g., 'T2', 'T4', 'C'). Used for connecting passenger MCT validation and ground handling coordination.
reference: IATA SSIM; SITA Airport Management Platform

description: Revenue management-approved total booking authorization for this leg, incorporating overbooking allowances above physical seat capacity. Set by Sabre AirVision Revenue Management and consumed by Altéa Inventory for availability control.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Overbooking Authorization; IATA RP 1724

description: UTC timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot for this leg as published to GDS and direct booking channels. Supports audit of availability state at a point in time and feeds through-availability calculations.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Snapshot; IATA ATA/B Standard

description: Current total number of confirmed bookings (Pax) held against this leg across all fare classes and cabins. Updated in real time by the Amadeus Altéa PSS as reservations are created, modified, or cancelled.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation and Inventory (PSS); IATA Resolution 787

description: Number of seats allocated to codeshare partner airlines on this operating leg. Represents inventory carved out for marketing carrier partners under codeshare agreements, reducing net availability for the operating carrier.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Codeshare Allocation

description: Flag indicating whether this leg is operated under a codeshare agreement, meaning one or more marketing carriers sell seats on this operating leg under their own flight numbers. True = codeshare arrangements exist for this leg.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare Agreements; DOT Codeshare Disclosure Rules

description: UTC timestamp when this inventory leg record was first created in the system, either by schedule publication or manual entry. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and compliance purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) — Accountability; ISO 9001:2015 — Records Control

description: Allocated airport departure slot time (UTC) assigned by the slot coordinator for this leg at the origin airport. May differ from scheduled departure time. Null for airports without slot coordination (non-Level 2/3 airports).
reference: IATA Worldwide Slot Guidelines (WSG); EC Regulation 95/93 (EU slot regulation)

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of this inventory leg (e.g., 'LAX', 'CDG', 'SIN'). Defines the physical destination point of the leg for inventory, pricing, and slot management.
reference: IATA Airport Codes Standard (IATA SSIM); ICAO Doc 7910

description: Number of seats currently blocked and held for group bookings on this leg. Reduces the net availability exposed to individual GDS and direct channel requests.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Inventory Control; IATA Resolution 787

description: Flag indicating whether this leg is eligible for interline ticketing, allowing passengers to be ticketed on a single document across multiple carriers. True = interline agreements apply to this leg.
reference: IATA Resolution 780 — Interline Traffic Agreements; IATA BSP

description: Date and time (UTC) when inventory for this leg was closed to new bookings. Typically triggered at departure or by revenue management closure rules. Null if inventory is still open.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Control; IATA Resolution 735

description: Date and time (UTC) when inventory for this leg was first opened for sale across GDS and direct channels. Marks the start of the revenue management availability window.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Control; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Flag indicating whether this leg is currently subject to an Irregular Operations (IROP) event such as a delay, cancellation, diversion, or equipment swap. True = IROP condition active. Triggers passenger re-accommodation workflows in Amadeus Altéa.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation and Inventory (PSS) — IROP Management; DOT 14 CFR Part 259

description: UTC timestamp of the most recent modification to this inventory leg record, including availability updates, status changes, capacity adjustments, or schedule revisions. Essential for incremental ETL processing and Silver Layer change detection.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) — Accountability; ISO 9001:2015 — Records Control; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: Current operational and inventory lifecycle status of this leg. 'active' — open for sale; 'cancelled' — flight cancelled, inventory closed; 'diverted' — flight diverted to alternate airport; 'suspended' — temporarily removed from sale; 'closed' — inventory closed post-departure.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Leg Status Codes; IATA Resolution 735

description: Current load factor for this leg expressed as a percentage: (booked_count / total_seat_capacity) × 100. Key revenue management KPI representing the percentage of available seats filled. Updated in real time as bookings change.
reference: IATA World Air Transport Statistics — Load Factor Definition; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: IATA day-of-week code on which this leg operates, where 1=Monday through 7=Sunday per IATA SSIM convention. Used for schedule frequency analysis and inventory pattern matching. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IATA SSIM Chapter 2 — Day of Week Coding

description: The overbooking multiplier applied to physical seat capacity for this leg, expressed as a percentage above 100 (e.g., 105.50 means 5.5% overbooking). Derived from Sabre AirVision Revenue Management no-show and cancellation forecasting models.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Overbooking Model; DOT 14 CFR Part 250 (Denied Boarding Compensation)

description: Scheduled gate arrival date and time for this leg in UTC (ISO 8601 format). Represents the planned OOOI 'In' time used for connection logic, MCT calculations, and inventory closure triggers.
reference: IATA SSIM Chapter 2 — Schedule Data; ICAO Annex 2

description: Planned block time for this leg in decimal hours, measured from scheduled gate departure (Out) to scheduled gate arrival (In). Block hours are the primary unit for aircraft utilization, crew duty time, and cost accounting (CASK calculations).
reference: IATA SSIM; FAA 14 CFR Part 117 — Flight and Duty Limitations; EASA ORO.FTL

description: Scheduled gate departure date and time for this leg in UTC (ISO 8601 format). Represents the planned OOOI 'Out' time used for inventory availability windows, GDS display, and OTP measurement.
reference: IATA SSIM Chapter 2 — Schedule Data; ICAO Annex 2

description: Great-circle distance of this leg in kilometres between origin and destination airports. Used in RPK (Revenue Passenger Kilometre) and ASK (Available Seat Kilometre) calculations, yield analysis, and CORSIA carbon emissions reporting.
reference: IATA World Air Transport Statistics — RPK/ASK Definitions; CORSIA (ICAO Doc 9501)

description: Ordinal position of this physical leg within the parent flight inventory record (e.g., 1 for the first leg, 2 for the second leg of a multi-leg service). Used to reconstruct itinerary order and support through-availability logic.
reference: IATA Resolution 787 — Leg/Segment Distinction; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: IATA single-character service type code indicating the nature of the flight service (e.g., 'J' for scheduled passenger, 'F' for scheduled freight, 'C' for charter). Drives inventory partitioning between passenger and cargo domains.
reference: IATA SSIM Appendix A — Service Type Codes

description: Flag indicating whether this leg participates in through-availability calculations, allowing connecting itinerary availability to be computed across multiple legs without individual leg-level availability checks. True = through-availability enabled.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Through Availability; IATA Resolution 787

description: Total number of sellable passenger seats on this leg across all cabins, as configured in the inventory system for the assigned aircraft type. This is the gross capacity before overbooking authorization is applied.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Capacity Management; IATA RP 1724

description: Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this leg, pending availability opening due to cancellations or capacity adjustments. Managed within Amadeus Altéa Inventory waitlist queues.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Management; IATA Resolution 735

description: Flag indicating whether this leg is operated under a wet lease (ACMI) arrangement, where the aircraft, crew, maintenance, and insurance are provided by a lessor airline. Affects operating carrier attribution and regulatory reporting.
reference: ICAO Doc 9626 — Wet Lease; EASA Air Operations Regulation (EU) 965/2012

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the overbooking control record. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Overbooking Control Entity

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: overbooking_control stores overbooking authorization parameters for a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links control parameters to the cabin mas

Foreign key linking to fleet.cabin_configuration. Business justification: Overbooking models are calibrated against authoritative physical seat capacity from cabin_configuration. physical_seat_capacity on overbooking_control is a denormalized copy prone to staleness after r

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Denied boarding compensation (voluntary and involuntary) is a direct cost tracked by cost center for financial reporting and DOT/regulatory compliance. Airlines monitor IDB costs against overbooking r

Reference to the flight inventory record (specific flight departure date and flight number combination) to which this overbooking control applies. Links to the sellable inventory unit in Altéa Inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Flight Inventory Object

description: Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Overbooking policies are route-specific, driven by route competitive intensity, no-show history, and regulatory market codes. Aviation revenue management and regulatory compliance require overbooking

Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: Seasonal no-show rates and overbooking model versions are calibrated per IATA schedule season. Season-level overbooking compliance reporting requires this FK. season_code is a denormalized season refe

Foreign key linking to revenue.yield_parameter. Business justification: Overbooking authorization rates are derived from yield parameters (no_show_rate, spoilage_tolerance, overbooking_authorization_rate). The RM system applies yield_parameter settings when computing over

The approved percentage above physical cabin seat capacity that the revenue management system is authorized to sell. For example, a value of 10.00 means up to 10% more seats than physically available may be booked. Derived from historical no-show rates and spoilage risk models in Sabre AirVision Revenue Management.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Overbooking Authorization; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: The timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot published to GDS and direct booking channels by Altéa Inventory. Indicates the currency of the current_booking_count and booking_limit values.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Real-Time Availability Distribution; IATA NDC

description: The average fare value in USD for bookings in this cabin on this flight, used as an input to the overbooking revenue optimization model. Enables the model to weigh the revenue benefit of additional bookings against the cost of denied boarding compensation.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Yield Optimization; IATA RP 1724

description: The computed maximum number of bookings permitted for this cabin on this flight, equal to physical seat capacity plus authorized oversale seats. This is the operative value consumed by Altéa Inventory and GDS availability responses to control seat sales.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Limit; IATA Resolution 728

description: Historical cancellation rate (expressed as a decimal proportion) for this route, cabin, and season used as an actuarial input alongside the no-show rate in the overbooking model. Distinct from no-show rate as it captures pre-departure cancellations.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Cancellation Forecasting

description: Indicates whether this overbooking control applies to a codeshare inventory allocation rather than the operating carrier's own inventory. Codeshare overbooking controls may differ from mainline controls due to interline agreements and partner airline seat block commitments.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Current lifecycle status of this overbooking control record. Active indicates the parameters are live and being applied by Altéa Inventory booking limit calculations. Suspended means overbooking is temporarily halted for this cabin. Overridden indicates a manual revenue management analyst override is in effect.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Limit Management; IATA Resolution 830d

description: The date and time when this overbooking control record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Record Audit; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: The real-time count of confirmed bookings (PNRs) held against this cabin at the time of the last availability snapshot. Used to compute remaining availability and monitor oversale exposure.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Real-Time Availability Snapshot

description: Bitmask or string pattern (e.g., '1234567' where each digit represents Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this overbooking control applies to. Allows differentiated overbooking levels for weekday versus weekend departures.
reference: IATA SSIM — Day of Week Encoding

description: The maximum acceptable probability (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0200 = 2%) that at least one passenger will be involuntarily denied boarding due to oversale. Balances revenue optimization against regulatory denied boarding compensation exposure under DOT 14 CFR Part 250 and IATA Resolution 830d.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250; IATA Resolution 830d; EASA Regulation EC 261/2004

description: The scheduled departure date of the flight to which this overbooking control record applies. Combined with the flight inventory reference, uniquely identifies the specific flight departure instance.
The date from which this overbooking control record becomes operative. Supports scheduling of future overbooking parameter changes in advance of the departure date.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Effective Date Management

description: The date after which this overbooking control record is no longer operative. Null indicates the record remains active indefinitely until superseded. Supports time-bounded overbooking strategies.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Effective Date Management

description: Historical rate of passengers who arrive at the gate without a prior reservation (go-shows) for this route and cabin, expressed as a decimal proportion. Used in the overbooking model to account for demand that materialises at departure.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Go-Show Demand Modelling

description: Number of seats within the cabin that are reserved for confirmed group bookings and excluded from the overbooking calculation. Group bookings typically have lower no-show rates and are treated separately in the overbooking model.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Inventory Management; IATA Resolution 728

description: The input no-show rate (expressed as a decimal proportion, e.g., 0.0850 = 8.50%) derived from historical passenger behaviour for this route, cabin, and season. Used as the primary actuarial input to the overbooking authorization model in Sabre AirVision.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — No-Show Forecasting; IATA RP 1724

description: Estimated maximum financial exposure in USD for involuntary denied boarding (IDB) compensation if all oversold seats result in IDB events. Calculated based on max_oversale_seats and applicable DOT/IATA compensation rates. Used in revenue management risk assessment.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250 — IDB Compensation Rates; IATA Resolution 830d

description: The date and time when a revenue management analyst last reviewed and confirmed or adjusted this overbooking control record. Supports governance workflows requiring periodic review of overbooking authorizations.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Review Workflow; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: The date and time when this overbooking control record was most recently modified. Tracks parameter changes by revenue management analysts or automated system recalculations.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Record Audit; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: The forecasted load factor (proportion of physical seats expected to be occupied at departure, expressed as a decimal e.g., 0.9200 = 92%) for this cabin on this flight. Input from Sabre AirVision demand forecasting, used to calibrate the overbooking level.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Demand Forecasting; IATA ASK/RPK Metrics

description: The absolute maximum number of seats above physical cabin capacity that may be sold, regardless of the authorized overbooking percentage. Acts as a hard cap to limit denied boarding exposure. Set by revenue management analysts in Altéa Inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Limit Cap; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: The scheduled date by which this overbooking control record must be reviewed by a revenue management analyst. Drives workflow queuing in Sabre AirVision to ensure timely recalibration before departure.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Review Scheduling

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes or context related to this overbooking control record, such as special event considerations, IROP recovery instructions, or coordination notes with ground handling teams.

Version identifier of the statistical overbooking model used to compute the authorized overbooking percentage for this record. Enables reproducibility, model governance, and comparison of model performance across versions in Sabre AirVision.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Model Versioning

description: Free-text justification provided by the revenue management analyst when rm_system_override_flag is True. Documents the business rationale for departing from the system-recommended overbooking level (e.g., special event, group booking, IROP recovery).
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Audit Trail; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code or regulatory jurisdiction code (e.g., USA, EU) identifying the regulatory regime governing denied boarding compensation obligations for this flight. Determines applicable DOT, EASA EC 261/2004, or national civil aviation authority rules.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250; EASA Regulation EC 261/2004; ICAO Annex 9; ISO 3166-1

description: Indicates whether a revenue management analyst has manually overridden the system-calculated overbooking parameters. When True, the authorized_overbooking_pct and max_oversale_seats reflect analyst-entered values rather than the automated Sabre AirVision model output.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Manual Override Controls

description: Identifies the originating system that created or last updated this overbooking control record. ALTEA = Amadeus Altéa Inventory automated calculation, SABRE_AV = Sabre AirVision Revenue Management model output, MANUAL = direct analyst entry.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: The maximum acceptable probability (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 = 5%) that the flight departs with empty seats due to under-booking. Revenue management uses this threshold to calibrate the overbooking level against the risk of seat spoilage versus denied boarding.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Spoilage/Denied Boarding Trade-off Model

description: Number of seats held back from the overbooking pool to accommodate anticipated operational upgrades (e.g., elite frequent flyer upgrades, compensation upgrades). Reduces the effective booking limit to preserve upgrade capacity.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Upgrade Management; Oracle Loyalty Cloud FFP

description: The target number of passengers expected to accept voluntary denied boarding (VDB) compensation offers at the gate in the event of an oversale. Used in the overbooking model to offset the involuntary denied boarding risk.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250 — Denied Boarding Compensation; IATA Resolution 830d

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the codeshare or interline inventory allocation record within the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Codeshare Allocation Object

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: codeshare_allocation defines seat allocations granted to codeshare partners for a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links allocation records to t

Foreign key linking to route.codeshare_agreement. Business justification: Codeshare seat allocations are governed by a codeshare agreement. Agreement compliance audits, revenue-share settlement, and regulatory approval tracking require linking each allocation to its governi

Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: codeshare_allocation carries a denormalized `fare_class_bucket` STRING column representing the RBD/booking class code for the allocation. fare_class_bucket is the authoritative master entity for booki
Reference to the specific flight-date inventory record in the host carrier's Altéa Inventory for which this codeshare allocation is ring-fenced.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Flight Inventory Object

description: Foreign key linking to finance.interline_billing. Business justification: Codeshare seat allocations with revenue_share_percentage and prorate_agreement_code directly generate interline billing settlements between operating and marketing carriers. IATA interline settlement
Reference to the airline that physically operates the flight on which this inventory allocation is granted. The operating carrier owns the aircraft and crew.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare Operations; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Codeshare inventory control requires linking each allocation to the operating carrier's authoritative flight number for GDS availability broadcasting and schedule-inventory reconciliation. operating_f

Foreign key linking to route.partnership. Business justification: Codeshare allocations execute the commercial terms of a partnership agreement. Partnership-level inventory reporting, prorate settlement, and block-space compliance require linking allocations directl

Current lifecycle state of the codeshare allocation record within Altéa Inventory. 'active' means the allocation is live and available for booking; 'suspended' means temporarily halted; 'cancelled' means permanently withdrawn; 'pending' means awaiting activation; 'expired' means the flight date has passed.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Allocation Lifecycle; IATA Resolution 788

description: Defines the inventory control mechanism governing this codeshare allocation. 'block_space' means a fixed seat block is ring-fenced for the marketing carrier regardless of overall availability; 'free_flow' means the marketing carrier draws from the operating carrier's shared availability pool without a hard block; 'hybrid' combines a guaranteed minimum block with free-flow access above it.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare Inventory Models; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Native allocation reference identifier assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system for this codeshare block. Used for reconciliation and traceability back to the operational PSS source record.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Codeshare Allocation Reference

description: Maximum number of seats the marketing carrier is authorised to sell on this flight-date and cabin under this allocation. For block-space agreements this is the contracted block size; for free-flow it represents the cap agreed in the bilateral.
reference: IATA Resolution 788; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Allocation Quantity

description: Timestamp of the most recent real-time availability snapshot captured from Altéa Inventory for this allocation. Used by GDS and direct channels to serve current seat availability to booking agents.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Real-Time Availability Feed; IATA NDC Standard

description: Human-readable external reference number or code of the bilateral codeshare/interline agreement (e.g., contract number from the commercial agreement). Stored for operational traceability; the full agreement detail is held in the bilateral_agreement master.
reference: IATA Resolution 788; IATA Interline Traffic Agreements

description: Timestamp when this codeshare allocation record was first created in the Altéa Inventory system, representing the business event of the allocation being established.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Audit Trail; ISO 8601

description: IATA-standard days-of-week string indicating on which days of the week this allocation applies within the effective date range (e.g., '1234567' = daily, '135' = Mon/Wed/Fri). Encoded as ISO weekday numbers 1=Monday through 7=Sunday.
reference: IATA SSIM (Standard Schedules Information Manual) — Days of Operation Encoding

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the operating flight segment covered by this allocation (e.g., CDG, SIN, ORD).
reference: IATA Airport Codes (IATA 3-Letter Codes); IATA Resolution 762

description: First date from which this codeshare allocation record is valid and active, as defined in the bilateral agreement. Supports seasonal schedule periods aligned with IATA scheduling seasons.
reference: IATA Resolution 788; IATA Scheduling Guidelines — Season Periods

description: Last date through which this codeshare allocation record is valid, as defined in the bilateral agreement. Null for open-ended allocations. Aligns with IATA scheduling season end dates.
reference: IATA Resolution 788; IATA Scheduling Guidelines — Season Periods

description: Scheduled departure date of the operating flight for which this codeshare allocation applies. Combined with the operating flight number, uniquely identifies the flight-date instance.

Flag indicating whether the marketing carrier's availability under this allocation is published and visible in Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport. When False, the allocation is restricted to direct or NDC channels only.
reference: IATA NDC Standard (PADIS); Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Channel Distribution

description: Flag indicating whether this allocation is governed by an interline ticketing agreement (True) rather than a pure codeshare arrangement (False). Interline allocations follow IATA BSP/ARC settlement rules.
reference: IATA Resolution 780 — Interline Traffic Agreements; IATA BSP Manual

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this codeshare allocation record, such as a change to seat counts, thresholds, or status. Supports audit trail and change tracking in the Silver layer.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Audit Trail; ISO 8601

description: Latest date on which the marketing carrier may sell the last available seat under this allocation before the booking window closes. May differ from the flight date due to advance purchase restrictions in the bilateral.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Window Controls; IATA Resolution 788

description: Reference to the partner airline that markets and sells seats under its own flight designator code on the operating carrier's flight. The marketing carrier holds this allocation.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare Operations; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: IATA-format flight designator under which the marketing carrier sells the codeshare seats (e.g., BA5678). This is the flight number visible to passengers booking through the marketing carrier.
reference: IATA Resolution 762 — Flight Designators; IATA Resolution 788

description: Hard upper limit on the number of seats the marketing carrier may sell on this flight-date and cabin, enforced by Altéa Inventory to prevent over-allocation beyond the bilateral agreement cap.
reference: IATA Resolution 788; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Allocation Cap

description: Minimum number of seats guaranteed to the marketing carrier that the operating carrier must hold available regardless of overall flight demand. Relevant for block-space and hybrid allocation types. Null for pure free-flow allocations.
reference: IATA Resolution 788; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Block Space Controls

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the operating flight segment covered by this allocation (e.g., LHR, JFK, DXB).
reference: IATA Airport Codes (IATA 3-Letter Codes); IATA Resolution 762

description: Multiplier applied to the authorized seat count to permit controlled overbooking of the marketing carrier's allocation (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% overbooking). Managed by the revenue management system in alignment with Sabre AirVision overbooking controls.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Overbooking Controls; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: Code referencing the revenue proration agreement between the operating and marketing carriers that governs how ticket revenue is split for seats sold under this codeshare allocation. Aligns with IATA BSP prorate rules.
reference: IATA Resolution 850 — Proration of Revenue; IATA BSP Manual

description: Date on which any unsold seats in a block-space allocation are automatically released back to the operating carrier's general inventory pool for unrestricted sale. Null for free-flow allocations where no block is held.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Block Release Provisions; Amadeus Altéa Inventory
