description: Number of days before scheduled departure at which unsold block-space seats are automatically released to the operating carrier's open inventory. Derived from the bilateral agreement terms and used to compute release_date.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Block Release Provisions; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Free-text operational notes or remarks associated with this codeshare allocation, such as special handling instructions, commercial exceptions, or temporary override justifications entered by inventory controllers.

Percentage of ticket revenue attributable to the operating carrier for seats sold by the marketing carrier under this allocation, as stipulated in the bilateral prorate agreement. Commercially sensitive.
reference: IATA Resolution 850 — Proration of Revenue; IATA BSP Manual

description: Current number of seats remaining available for sale by the marketing carrier under this allocation (authorized_seat_count minus seats_sold, subject to overbooking controls). Reflects the real-time availability figure published to GDS and direct channels.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Real-Time Availability; IATA NDC Standard

description: Current count of seats already sold or confirmed by the marketing carrier against this allocation as of the last inventory snapshot. Used to compute remaining availability and monitor allocation utilisation.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Real-Time Availability Snapshot

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the group block record in the inventory system. Primary key for this entity.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_receivable. Business justification: Group blocks carry deposit_amount, deposit_paid, deposit_paid_date, and final_payment_due_date — all generating AR records. Finance tracks group booking deposits and final payments as receivables. Rev

Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: group_block allocates seats in a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links group block records to the cabin master. cabin_code is redundant and ava

Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: group_block carries a denormalized `booking_class_code` STRING column representing the RBD code for the group allocation. fare_class_bucket is the authoritative master entity for booking class buckets
Reference to the fare class bucket (booking class) under which the group block seats are held. Determines the yield and revenue management bucket impacted by this block.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Fare Class Bucket; IATA Resolution 728

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: group_block reserves a seat block for a group booking on a specific flight-date. flight_inventory is the master inventory record for that flight-date. This FK establishes the natural parent-child rela

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_leg. Business justification: group_block currently links to flight.flight_leg (cross-domain operational leg) but lacks a link to inventory.inventory_leg (the inventory management unit). For multi-leg itineraries, group blocks mus
Reference to the master Passenger Name Record (PNR) associated with this group block. A group block is linked to a single group PNR in the Passenger Service System (PSS).
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — PNR; IATA Resolution 722

description: Current lifecycle status of the group block. 'tentative' = seats held pending confirmation; 'confirmed' = group has committed; 'ticketed' = tickets issued; 'cancelled' = block released back to inventory; 'expired' = option date passed without confirmation; 'waitlisted' = requested but not yet allocated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tentative|confirmed|ticketed|cancelled|expired|waitlisted — promote to reference product]
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Status; IATA Resolution 722

description: Indicates whether this group block is associated with a codeshare flight operation, where the operating carrier differs from the marketing carrier. True = codeshare flight; False = own-metal operation. Affects inventory allocation and interline settlement.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 — Codeshare; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: The timestamp when this group block record was first created in the inventory system. Supports audit trail, SLA tracking, and data lineage requirements.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); ISO 9001

description: The monetary deposit amount required or received for this group block, expressed in fare_currency_code. Null if deposit_required is False.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Deposit; IATA Resolution 722; BSP Settlement

description: Indicates whether the required deposit has been received from the group organizer. True = deposit received; False = deposit outstanding. Drives follow-up and cancellation workflows.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Deposit Status; IATA Resolution 722

description: The date on which the deposit payment was received from the group organizer. Null if deposit has not been paid.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Deposit; BSP Settlement

description: Indicates whether a deposit payment is required from the group organizer to hold or confirm this group block. True = deposit required; False = no deposit required.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Deposit; IATA Resolution 722

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the group_fare_amount is denominated (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP').
The date by which the full fare payment for all ticketed seats in the group block must be settled. Distinct from the deposit due date and ticketing deadline.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Payment; BSP Settlement; ARC Reporting

description: The ratio of complimentary (free) seats granted to the group organizer relative to the total seats purchased, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1000 = 1 free seat per 10 paid). Drives revenue accounting and yield calculations.
reference: IATA Resolution 722 — Group Complimentary Seats; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: The actual number of complimentary (free) seats allocated to the group organizer based on the free_seat_ratio and confirmed group size. Impacts revenue accounting and load factor reporting.
reference: IATA Resolution 722 — Group Complimentary Seats; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Negotiated per-seat fare amount agreed for this group block, expressed in the currency specified by fare_currency_code. Represents the contracted price per passenger for revenue accounting and settlement.
reference: IATA Resolution 722 — Group Fares; BSP Settlement; DOT Reporting

description: The fare basis code applicable to the group block, defining the fare rules, restrictions, and pricing conditions under which the group seats are sold (e.g., 'GEXCAP', 'YGROUP'). Aligns with IATA fare construction rules.
reference: IATA Resolution 722 — Fare Basis; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: The commercial or organizational name of the group for whom the seat block is reserved (e.g., 'Smith Wedding Party', 'ACME Corp Sales Conference'). Used for identification and communication with the group organizer.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Master; IATA Resolution 722

description: Classification of the group by purpose or segment. Used for revenue management analysis, pricing strategy, and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: leisure|corporate|incentive|military|religious|sports|charter — promote to reference product]
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Segment Classification; IATA Resolution 722

description: The timestamp when this group block record was most recently updated in the inventory system. Used for change detection, ETL delta processing, and audit compliance.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); ISO 9001

description: The minimum number of passengers required to qualify for group fare pricing on this block, as defined by the applicable fare rules. Typically 10 passengers per IATA group fare conventions.
reference: IATA Resolution 722 — Group Fare Minimum; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: The date by which the group organizer must submit individual passenger names (Pax names) for all seats in the confirmed block. Required for check-in, security compliance, and advance passenger information (API) submission.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — Group Names Due; IATA Resolution 722; TSA Secure Flight; ICAO Annex 9

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (relevant for codeshare blocks where the operating carrier may differ from the marketing carrier). Required for interline settlement and ACARS reporting.
reference: IATA Resolution 762 — Airline Designators; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: The deadline date by which the group organizer must confirm the number of seats and provide passenger names. If not confirmed by this date, the block may be automatically released back to inventory. Critical for inventory management and revenue optimization.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Option Date; IATA Resolution 722

description: Indicates whether this group block is subject to overbooking controls. True = the block counts against overbooking limits; False = the block is protected from overbooking displacement (e.g., guaranteed allocation). Feeds revenue management overbooking models.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Overbooking Controls; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this group block by the Passenger Service System (PSS) or reservations team. Used by travel agents and group coordinators to identify and manage the block.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Reference; IATA Resolution 722

description: Free-text remarks or special instructions associated with this group block, entered by the reservations agent or group coordinator. May include special service requests (SSR), handling notes, or commercial conditions.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — PNR Remarks; IATA Resolution 722

description: The distribution channel through which the group block was requested and booked. 'direct' = airline reservations team; 'gds' = Global Distribution System (GDS) such as Amadeus or Sabre; 'online_travel_agency' = OTA; 'corporate' = corporate travel desk; 'charter' = charter operator.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — Distribution Channel; IATA Resolution 722; GDS Standards

description: Current number of seats held (blocked) in inventory for this group. Reduces available seat count for individual sale. May differ from seats_requested if the airline partially approved the block.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Allocation; IATA Resolution 722

description: Number of seats within the block that have been formally confirmed by the group organizer. May be less than seats_blocked during the tentative phase. Drives revenue recognition and ticketing obligations.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Confirmation; IATA Resolution 722

description: Number of seats that have been released back to general inventory from this group block, either due to partial cancellation, reduction, or expiry of the option. Supports inventory reconciliation.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block Release; IATA Resolution 722

description: Total number of seats originally requested by the group organizer at the time of the initial group block request. Baseline for tracking changes to the block size.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Group Block; IATA Resolution 722

description: Number of seats within the confirmed block for which electronic tickets (e-tickets) have been issued. Used to track ticketing progress against the ticketing deadline.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — e-Ticket; IATA Resolution 722; BSP Settlement

description: The final date by which all confirmed seats in the group block must be ticketed (e-tickets issued). Failure to ticket by this date may result in automatic cancellation of unticketed seats and release to general inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — Ticketing Deadline; IATA Resolution 722; BSP Settlement

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each seat availability snapshot record in the inventory system. Primary key for this data product.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Availability Snapshot Entity

description: Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: seat_availability carries a denormalized `cabin_class` STRING column representing the cabin class code. cabin_class is a first-class master entity in the inventory domain. Adding cabin_class_id as a d

Foreign key linking to inventory.fare_class_bucket. Business justification: seat_availability tracks availability for a specific fare class bucket (RBD). fare_class_bucket is the master entity defining RBD buckets. This FK links transactional availability records to the bucke

Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: seat_availability records are real-time availability snapshots for a specific flight-date combination. flight_inventory is the master entity for that flight-date. This FK establishes the natural paren
Reference to the operating flight record for which seat availability is being tracked. Links to the flight master entity.

Current booking status of the fare class bucket as broadcast to distribution channels. Open = seats available for sale; Closed = bucket closed by revenue management; Waitlist = waitlist accepted; Request = booking requires airline confirmation; Sold Out = no seats remain. Core inventory control signal consumed by GDS, NDC, and direct channels.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; IATA Resolution 728; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Monotonically increasing version counter for this flight-date-fare-class availability record. Enables ordering of multiple snapshots for the same key and supports optimistic concurrency control in the Silver layer.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

description: Availability status string (AVS) representing the full cabin availability across all booking classes at the time of snapshot, as broadcast in GDS availability responses (e.g., 'Y9B9M9Q4V0'). Enables reconstruction of the complete availability display seen by travel agents.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa Inventory; SITA AIRIMP

description: Date and time when this availability update was broadcast to GDS and distribution channels. Distinct from snapshot_timestamp (capture time) and last_updated_timestamp (system update time). Used for GDS broadcast latency measurement and SLA verification.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa Inventory; SITA AIRIMP

description: Number of seats allocated to the codeshare marketing partner within this fare class bucket. Null when codeshare_indicator is false. Used to track partner inventory consumption against agreed allotment caps.

Flag indicating whether this availability record pertains to a codeshare flight operation where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier. When true, inventory is managed under a codeshare allocation agreement.
Date and time when this availability snapshot record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for Silver layer data lineage and regulatory compliance.
reference: IATA Resolution 787; CORSIA; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions

description: Number of calendar days between the snapshot_timestamp date and the flight_date. Critical dimension for revenue management booking curve analysis and yield optimization model training.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Channel context for which this availability snapshot was generated or broadcast. GDS = Global Distribution System (Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport); NDC = IATA New Distribution Capability; Direct = airline.com or mobile app; API = B2B direct connect; Interline = partner carrier ticketing; Codeshare = shared flight operation partner.
reference: IATA NDC Standard (PADIS); IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Revenue management system's demand forecast for this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot, expressed as expected number of bookings. Captured alongside actual availability for model accuracy tracking and yield optimization.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: IATA-assigned GDS provider code identifying the specific GDS to which this availability was broadcast: 1A = Amadeus, 1S = Sabre, 1G = Galileo (Travelport), 1P = Worldspan (Travelport). Null when distribution_channel is not GDS.
reference: IATA GDS Provider Codes; IATA Resolution 787

description: Method by which inventory is controlled for this availability record: Leg = physical leg-based control; Segment = segment-level control; OD = Origin-Destination (virtual nesting / O&D control). Determines how Amadeus Altéa manages seat allocation across connecting itineraries.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Flag indicating whether this record represents the most recent (current) availability snapshot for the flight-date-fare-class combination. Enables efficient point-in-time queries without full history scan. Set to false when superseded by a newer snapshot.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer conventions; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Flag indicating whether this fare class bucket is open for interline ticketing by partner carriers under bilateral interline agreements. Governs availability visibility in interline booking flows.

Date and time when this availability record was last modified in the source inventory system (Amadeus Altéa). Used for GDS broadcast verification, change detection, and Silver layer incremental processing.

Percentage of authorized seats sold at the time of this snapshot, calculated as (seats_sold / seats_authorized) × 100. Captured at snapshot time for historical revenue management model training and GDS broadcast audit. Aligns with IATA load factor (percentage of seats filled) definition.
reference: IATA World Air Transport Statistics; DOT Form 41; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier under whose flight number the seat is sold to passengers. On non-codeshare flights this equals the operating carrier code.
reference: IATA Airline Designator Standard; IATA Resolution 788

description: Hierarchical nesting position of this fare class bucket within the cabin's virtual nesting structure used by the revenue management system. Lower values indicate higher-yield buckets. Governs availability inheritance between fare classes.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier physically operating the flight. Differs from the marketing carrier on codeshare flights. Required for interline and codeshare inventory allocation tracking.
reference: IATA Airline Designator Standard; IATA Resolution 788

description: Authorized overbooking multiplier applied to physical seat capacity for this fare class bucket, expressed as a percentage above 100 (e.g., 105.00 = 5% overbooking). Set by revenue management to compensate for expected no-shows and cancellations.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT 14 CFR Part 250 (Oversales)

description: Total number of physical seats configured in the cabin for this flight-date, before overbooking authorization. Provides the denominator for cabin-level load factor calculations and capacity planning.

Number of seats protected (reserved) for higher-yield fare classes above this bucket in the nesting hierarchy, as set by the revenue management optimizer. Core EMSR (Expected Marginal Seat Revenue) output used in Altéa and Sabre inventory control.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Code identifying the revenue management optimization decision or rule that triggered the current availability status or booking limit for this fare class bucket (e.g., FCST_CLOSE, MANUAL_OVERRIDE, AUTO_OPEN). Supports RM model audit and performance attribution.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Best Practices

description: Maximum number of bookings authorized for this fare class bucket as set by the revenue management system. Represents the booking limit or allotment cap controlling inventory exposure for this RBD.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory; IATA Resolution 728

description: Number of seats currently available for sale in this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. A value of zero with status Open may indicate overbooking authorization. Negative values indicate oversold position.

Number of seats temporarily held under option, group block, or tour operator allotment that are not yet confirmed bookings but reduce net availability. Distinct from seats_sold as these may be released.
Number of confirmed bookings (including checked-in passengers) held against this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. Used to compute load factor and remaining availability.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; DOT Form 41 Traffic Statistics

description: Exact date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which this availability record was captured or broadcast. The principal business event timestamp for this EVENT_LOG record. Used for GDS broadcast verification and revenue management time-series analysis.

Business event or system action that caused this availability snapshot to be generated and broadcast. Enables audit of why availability changed at a given point in time. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Booking|Cancellation|Schedule Change|RM Override|GDS Poll|NDC Request|Overbooking Adjustment|Batch Interval — promote to reference product]
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this availability record: ALTEA = Amadeus Altéa Inventory (PSS); SABRE = Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; NETLINE = Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops; MANUAL = manually entered override.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this fare class bucket at the time of the snapshot. Relevant when availability_status is Waitlist. Used to assess demand pressure and inform overbooking decisions.
Unique surrogate identifier for the commercial Origin-Destination (O&D) inventory segment record in the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Segment-level inventory control and nesting logic depend on aircraft type's seat capacity and cabin class distribution. GDS availability calculations and codeshare seat allocation require aircraft typ

Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: inventory_segment represents a commercial O&D segment sold in a specific cabin class. cabin_class is the reference master for cabin definitions. This FK links segment records to the cabin master. cabi

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_class. Business justification: Inventory segments are controlled by fare class rules (availability_control_method, booking_class). segment has booking_class and fare_basis_code as plain attributes but no fare_class_id FK. Availabil

Foreign key linking to route.flight_number. Business justification: Codeshare inventory management and GDS schedule-segment reconciliation require segments to reference their authoritative flight number. marketed_flight_number is a denormalized string; this FK normali

Foreign key linking to finance.interline_billing. Business justification: Interline-eligible segments (interline_eligible=true, distinct operating_carrier_code vs marketing_carrier_code) are the unit of IATA interline proration and billing. Finance interline settlement team
Reference to the underlying physical inventory leg (flight leg) from which this commercial segment derives its availability. A segment may span one or more legs in a connecting itinerary.

Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Revenue management bucket configuration and network planning reports are route-specific. Aviation RM analysts expect segments to be directly traceable to their route for yield, RASK, and competitive i

Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: RM bucket open/close cycles and seasonal demand forecasting are calibrated per IATA schedule season. Linking segments to schedule_season enables season-over-season yield comparison. season_code is a d
Reference to the scheduled flight record associated with this inventory segment, linking to the flight operations domain for operational context.

Total number of seats authorized for sale on this segment in the specified cabin and booking class, including any overbooking allowance approved by revenue management. This is the gross sellable inventory before netting sold seats.

Method used to control seat availability for this segment: 'leg_based' = availability derived from individual leg bucket counts; 'od_based' = availability controlled at the O&D segment level using virtual nesting; 'hybrid' = combination of leg and O&D controls.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Timestamp (UTC) at which the current availability figures (seats_sold, seats_available, load_factor) were last refreshed from the Amadeus Altéa Inventory system. Critical for GDS cache synchronization and real-time availability accuracy.

Single-letter IATA Reservation Booking Designator (RBD) representing the fare class bucket for this segment (e.g., Y, B, M, Q, V, W). Directly controls availability nesting and revenue management yield optimization in Sabre AirVision and Amadeus Altéa.

Number of seats allocated to the marketing carrier under a block-space codeshare agreement for this segment. Applicable only when codeshare_type = 'block_space'. Null for free-flow or non-codeshare segments.

Type of codeshare arrangement governing inventory allocation for this segment: 'free_flow' = marketing carrier draws from operating carrier's open inventory; 'block_space' = a fixed seat block is allocated to the marketing carrier; 'none' = not a codeshare segment.
Timestamp (UTC) when this inventory segment record was first created in the system, typically when the flight schedule was loaded into Amadeus Altéa Inventory for the season.

Scheduled departure date of the segment at the origin airport in local time (yyyy-MM-dd). Used as the primary date dimension for inventory availability queries and revenue management yield optimization.
Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination station of this commercial O&D segment as sold to the passenger. Defines the arrival point of the sellable unit.

IATA fare basis code identifying the specific fare product associated with this inventory segment (e.g., YLOWUS, BAPOW). Governs fare rules, restrictions, and the booking class bucket to which availability is mapped.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; IATA Fare Construction Rules

description: Availability status code published to Global Distribution Systems (GDS) such as Amadeus, Sabre, and Travelport for this segment and booking class. Standard codes include numeric seat counts (0-9) or alpha codes (A=available, C=closed, L=waitlist) per IATA AIRIMP standards.
reference: IATA AIRIMP; IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa Inventory

description: Number of seats currently held under group booking reservations for this segment. Group holds reduce available inventory and are subject to release deadlines managed by the group desk.

Indicates whether this inventory segment is eligible for interline ticketing agreements, allowing passengers to book connecting itineraries across multiple carriers on a single ticket. Governed by bilateral interline agreements and IATA BSP/ARC settlement rules.
reference: IATA Resolution 780; IATA BSP Manual; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Current load factor for this inventory segment expressed as a percentage of physical cabin capacity that is sold or confirmed (seats_sold / physical_capacity * 100). Key performance indicator for revenue management and capacity planning. Note: this is a point-in-time snapshot value, not a computed aggregate.
reference: IATA Economics; DOT Form 41; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier that markets and sells this segment to passengers. For codeshare flights, this is the airline whose flight number appears on the ticket.
reference: IATA Airline Coding Directory; IATA Resolution 788

description: Minimum Connecting Time (MCT) in minutes required at the connecting airport for this segment to be valid in a connecting itinerary. Enforced during availability display and booking to prevent infeasible connections. Sourced from IATA SSIM MCT tables.

Hierarchical nesting level of this booking class within the cabin's availability nesting structure (e.g., 1=highest yield class, 10=lowest yield class). Governs through-availability rules: higher-level classes inherit availability from lower-level buckets per revenue management nesting policy.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; Amadeus Altéa Inventory; IATA Resolution 728

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier that physically operates the aircraft for this segment. Differs from marketing_carrier_code on codeshare flights.
Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin station of this commercial O&D segment as sold to the passenger. Defines the departure point of the sellable unit.

Maximum number of seats that may be sold beyond the physical cabin capacity for this segment, as authorized by revenue management overbooking controls. Expressed as an absolute seat count above physical capacity.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT 14 CFR Part 250; IATA Resolution 787

description: Overbooking authorization expressed as a percentage above physical cabin capacity (e.g., 5.00 = 5% overbooking). Used by revenue management to calibrate no-show risk models and optimize load factor.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: Date after which this inventory segment is no longer available for new bookings. Typically set to the departure date or earlier based on advance purchase restrictions and revenue management close-out rules.
Date from which this inventory segment is open for sale in GDS and direct booking channels. Defines the advance purchase window start for revenue management and distribution control.
Scheduled arrival date and time of the segment at the destination airport in UTC (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for MCT enforcement and connecting itinerary feasibility validation.

Scheduled departure date and time of the segment at the origin airport in UTC (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for precise GDS availability display and MCT (Minimum Connecting Time) enforcement calculations.

Current count of seats available for sale on this segment, computed as authorized_capacity minus seats_sold minus seats_blocked. This is the real-time availability figure published to GDS and direct channels.

Number of seats held back from general sale for operational purposes such as crew positioning, group holds, codeshare allocations, or special service requests (SSR). Reduces net available inventory without being sold.
Number of seats currently confirmed as sold (booked and ticketed) on this inventory segment. Updated in real-time from the Passenger Service System (PSS) as bookings are made or cancelled.

Current availability status of this inventory segment as published to GDS and direct booking channels. 'open' = seats available for sale; 'closed' = no availability; 'waitlisted' = demand exceeds supply, waitlist active; 'cancelled' = flight/segment cancelled; 'suspended' = temporarily removed from sale.

Classification of the commercial segment by its operational and commercial structure: 'direct' = single leg O&D; 'connecting' = multi-leg through-availability segment; 'codeshare' = marketed by partner airline; 'interline' = cross-carrier ticketing agreement; 'virtual' = constructed availability for display purposes.

Indicates whether this segment participates in through-availability nesting, meaning its availability is derived from the aggregated availability of underlying leg buckets rather than being independently controlled. True = through-availability applies.

Timestamp (UTC) of the most recent update to this inventory segment record, including availability changes, overbooking adjustments, or status transitions. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.
Number of passengers currently on the waitlist for this inventory segment and booking class. Used by revenue management to assess latent demand and inform overbooking and upgrade decisions.
Unique surrogate identifier for each waitlist entry record in the Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. Primary key for this data product.

Foreign key linking to reservation.reservation_booking_passenger. Business justification: Waitlist clearance workflow requires identifying the specific passenger (not just PNR) to notify, upgrade, and trigger boarding pass issuance. A PNR can have multiple passengers; without this FK, the
Reference to the specific fare class booking class bucket (e.g., Y, B, M, Q) for which availability was closed and the waitlist entry was created. Drives revenue management yield optimization.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Class Bucket; IATA Resolution 728

description: Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_class. Business justification: Waitlist entries specify the requested fare class (RBD) for clearance processing. Waitlist clearance algorithms prioritize by fare class yield, eligibility rules, and bucket authorization. Essential f
Reference to the specific flight inventory record (flight-date-cabin combination) for which this waitlist entry is held. Identifies the sellable inventory unit in Altéa Inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Flight Inventory Object

description: Reference to the group booking record when the waitlist entry originates from a group overflow scenario (waitlist_reason = GROUP_OVERFLOW). Links the individual waitlist entry back to the parent group contract for group management reporting.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — Group Booking; IATA Resolution 728

description: Reference to the individual passenger profile associated with this waitlist entry. A single PNR may contain multiple passengers, each generating a separate waitlist entry.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — Passenger Profile; IATA Resolution 722

description: Reference to the Passenger Name Record (PNR) booking that is placed on the waitlist. Links the waitlist entry to the originating reservation in the Passenger Service System (PSS).
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — PNR Object; IATA Resolution 722

description: Native waitlist sequence number assigned by the Amadeus Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. Serves as the business identifier for this waitlist entry within the Altéa system, used for reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the source PSS.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Sequence; IATA Resolution 728

description: Single-letter IATA booking class (fare class) code within the requested cabin for which the waitlist entry was created (e.g., Y, B, M, H, Q, K, L, V, W, S, N, G). Directly maps to a revenue management fare class bucket in Altéa Inventory.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Booking Class

description: IATA single-character cabin designator for the cabin class requested by the waitlisted passenger. F: First Class; C: Business Class; W: Premium Economy; Y: Economy. Determines the inventory pool from which clearance will be attempted.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Cabin Configuration

description: Distribution channel through which the waitlist entry was created. GDS: Global Distribution System (e.g., Amadeus, Sabre, Travelport); NDC: IATA New Distribution Capability channel; DIRECT_WEB: airline direct website; MOBILE: airline mobile application; AIRPORT: airport check-in or gate agent; CALL_CENTER: reservations call center; INTERLINE: interline partner booking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDS|NDC|DIRECT_WEB|MOBILE|AIRPORT|CALL_CENTER|INTERLINE — promote to reference product]

The actual booking class in which the waitlist entry was cleared and confirmed, which may differ from the originally requested booking class if the revenue management system cleared the passenger into a higher-availability bucket. Null until clearance occurs.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Clearance; IATA Resolution 728

description: Method by which the waitlist entry was cleared. AUTOMATIC: Altéa Inventory engine cleared automatically when bucket availability opened; MANUAL: airport or reservations agent manually cleared the entry; UPGRADE_PROCESSING: cleared as part of an upgrade processing run; IROP_REACCOMMODATION: cleared during Irregular Operations (IROP) reaccommodation workflow.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Clearance Method; IATA Resolution 728

description: Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry was successfully cleared and the seat confirmed. Null if the entry has not yet been cleared. Used to measure waitlist clearance latency and revenue recovery timing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Clearance Event; IATA Resolution 728

description: Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry was first created in the Altéa Inventory system. Serves as the business event timestamp for queue ordering and audit purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Creation Event; IATA Resolution 722

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the arrival airport of the waitlisted flight segment (e.g., LAX, CDG, SIN). Used for route-level waitlist analysis and GDS availability messaging.
reference: IATA Airport Codes Standard; IATA Resolution 728

description: Date and time (UTC) at which the waitlist entry will automatically expire if not cleared, typically aligned with the flight departure time or a configurable pre-departure deadline set by revenue management policy. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Expiry Rules; IATA Resolution 728

description: IATA fare basis code associated with the waitlisted booking (e.g., YLOWUS, BAPUS21). Encodes the fare family, booking class, and applicable restrictions. Used by revenue management to validate clearance eligibility and by pricing to re-price upon confirmation.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Fare Basis

description: Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) membership number of the waitlisted passenger. Used to apply loyalty-based priority rules and to credit miles upon clearance and travel. Classified as confidential PII as it is a unique personal identifier.
reference: Oracle Loyalty Cloud — Member Number; IATA Resolution 728; GDPR Article 4

description: Frequent Flyer Program (FFP) membership tier of the waitlisted passenger at the time of waitlist entry creation (e.g., NONE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, ELITE). Higher tiers receive elevated priority rank in the waitlist queue per revenue management policy.
reference: Oracle Loyalty Cloud — FFP Tier; IATA Resolution 728; DOT 14 CFR Part 253

description: Scheduled local departure date of the flight for which the waitlist entry is held. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Used for inventory snapshot reporting and waitlist aging analysis.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Flight Date

description: IATA flight number (carrier designator + numeric suffix, e.g., AA101) for the flight on which the waitlist entry is held. Denormalized from the flight inventory record for operational reporting and GDS availability messaging.

Queue number assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) for this waitlist entry, used to route clearance notifications back to the originating travel agent or booking system. Null for direct-channel bookings.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation — GDS Queue Management; IATA Resolution 722

description: Indicates whether the waitlist entry is associated with a codeshare flight operation where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier. True: codeshare booking; False: own-metal operation. Affects inventory allotment rules and clearance authority.
reference: IATA Resolution 788 (Codeshare); Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Codeshare Inventory

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier under whose flight number the ticket was sold (marketing carrier). For codeshare flights, this may differ from the operating carrier. Determines which inventory pool and allotment rules apply.

Indicates whether a clearance or status-change notification has been dispatched to the passenger or booking agent following a waitlist status transition. True: notification sent; False: notification pending or not applicable. Supports DOT consumer notification compliance.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 259 — Passenger Notification; IATA Resolution 722

description: Date and time (UTC) when the clearance or status-change notification was dispatched to the passenger or booking agent. Null if no notification has been sent. Used for DOT consumer protection compliance reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 259 — Passenger Notification; IATA Resolution 722

description: Two-character IATA airline designator of the carrier actually operating the flight (e.g., AA, BA, EK). Relevant for codeshare flights where the marketing carrier differs from the operating carrier.
reference: IATA Airline Designator Standard; ICAO Doc 8585

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the departure airport of the waitlisted flight segment (e.g., JFK, LHR, DXB). Used for route-level waitlist analysis and GDS availability messaging.
reference: IATA Airport Codes Standard; IATA Resolution 728

description: Numeric rank of this waitlist entry within the queue for the same flight, cabin, and fare class bucket. Lower values indicate higher priority for clearance. Determined by Altéa Inventory based on booking time, fare paid, and frequent flyer tier.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Queue Management; IATA Resolution 728

description: Indicates whether a revenue management analyst manually overrode the standard Altéa Inventory waitlist clearance rules for this entry (e.g., to prioritise a high-value passenger or honour a corporate contract). True: manual override applied; False: standard automated rules applied.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Override Controls; IATA Resolution 728

description: Number of seats requested on the waitlist for this entry. For individual bookings this is typically 1; for group bookings this may be greater than 1. Determines the inventory units required for clearance.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Seat Request; IATA Resolution 728

description: Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this waitlist entry. Primarily ALTEA_INV (Amadeus Altéa Inventory) but may include GDS source codes for externally originated waitlist requests. Used for data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — System Integration; IATA Resolution 728

description: Comma-separated list of IATA Special Service Request (SSR) codes associated with the waitlisted passenger (e.g., WCHR, VGML, UMNR). These SSRs must be honoured upon clearance and seat assignment. Stored as a denormalized string for operational reference.
reference: IATA Resolution 722 — SSR Codes; Amadeus Altéa Reservation — SSR

description: 13-digit IATA electronic ticket (e-ticket) number associated with the waitlisted booking segment. Follows the IATA 13-digit format (3-digit airline code + 10-digit serial). May be null if the ticket has not yet been issued at the time of waitlist creation.

Date and time (UTC) when the waitlist entry record was last modified in the source system, including status transitions, priority re-ranking, or clearance processing. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Record Audit; ISO 8601

description: For upgrade-request waitlist entries, the cabin code of the originally booked cabin from which the passenger is requesting an upgrade (e.g., Y for Economy). Null for sold-out or group overflow waitlist reasons. Used by upgrade processing logic in Altéa Inventory.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Upgrade Processing; IATA Resolution 728

description: Business reason why the passenger or booking was placed on the waitlist rather than confirmed. SOLD_OUT: requested fare class bucket was closed; UPGRADE_REQUEST: passenger requested cabin upgrade pending availability; GROUP_OVERFLOW: group booking exceeded available allotment; OVERBOOKING_CONTROL: revenue management overbooking limit reached; CODESHARE_ALLOTMENT: codeshare partner inventory allocation exhausted.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Reason Codes; IATA Resolution 728

description: Current lifecycle status of the waitlist entry as managed by the Altéa Inventory waitlist processing engine. OPEN: actively queued awaiting availability; CLEARED: bucket opened and seat confirmed; CANCELLED: passenger or agent cancelled the waitlist request; EXPIRED: flight departed or deadline passed without clearance; REJECTED: system or revenue management rule prevented clearance.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Inventory — Waitlist Status Codes; IATA Resolution 728

description: Unique identifier for the Part-145 approved maintenance organisation record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: External MROs generate AP invoices for services rendered. Links MRO to their invoices for vendor payment tracking, spend analysis, and contract compliance verification.

Foreign key linking to safety.audit. Business justification: Maintenance organizations are audited (Part-145 audits, IOSA). Link tracks audit history and findings against specific MROs, supports vendor quality management, and enables audit-driven MRO selection

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In multi-entity airline groups, AMO contracts are held by specific legal entities/company codes for intercompany accounting and tax purposes. Aviation finance requires AMO-to-company-code mapping for

Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: AMOs operate at specific airport stations; this link supports station-level maintenance capability queries, AOG response planning, and contract management. Aviation experts expect AMOs to be formally
Name of the accountable manager responsible for ensuring the maintenance organisation complies with Part-145 requirements.
Primary street address line of the maintenance organisation facility.
Secondary street address line (building, suite, floor) of the maintenance organisation facility.
Indicates whether the organisation holds airframe maintenance rating approval.

business_glossary_term: Aircraft on Ground (AOG) Support Available Flag
description: Indicates whether the maintenance organisation provides 24/7 AOG (Aircraft on Ground) emergency support services.
Date when the Part-145 approval certificate expires and requires renewal. Null if approval has no fixed expiry (continuous validity subject to surveillance).
Date when the Part-145 approval certificate was originally issued by the regulatory authority.
Official Part-145 approval certificate number issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., EASA.145.1234, FAA-RS-ABC123).

Textual description of the maintenance capabilities and ratings covered by the approval (e.g., 'Airframe rating: Boeing 737 series, Airbus A320 family; Engine rating: CFM56-7B; Component rating: Landing gear, hydraulics').
Current regulatory status of the Part-145 approval certificate: active, suspended, expired, revoked, or pending renewal.
Indicates whether the organisation holds Auxiliary Power Unit (APU) maintenance rating approval.
Number of open audit findings or corrective action requests from the last audit.
Indicates whether the organisation holds avionics and electrical systems maintenance rating approval.
Reference identifier or document number for the detailed capability list (CoA appendix) that enumerates specific aircraft types, engines, and components the organisation is approved to maintain.
City where the maintenance organisation facility is located.
Indicates whether the organisation holds component maintenance rating approval (e.g., landing gear, avionics, hydraulics).
Primary contact email address for the maintenance organisation.
Primary contact telephone number for the maintenance organisation.
Expiry or termination date of the commercial maintenance services contract. Null for open-ended contracts.
Effective start date of the commercial maintenance services contract.
Status of the commercial contract with the third-party MRO provider: active, inactive, pending, or terminated. Not applicable for internal maintenance organisations.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the maintenance organisation is registered and operates (e.g., DEU, USA, GBR).
Timestamp when this approved maintenance organisation record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the organisation holds engine maintenance rating approval.

National or regional aviation authority that issued the Part-145 approval certificate (e.g., EASA, FAA, CAAC, TCCA, CASA, ANAC, DGCA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EASA|FAA|CAAC|TCCA|CASA|ANAC|DGCA — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ICAO Annex 6, EASA Part-145, FAA Part-145

description: Date of the most recent regulatory or internal audit conducted at the maintenance organisation.
Timestamp when this approved maintenance organisation record was last modified.
Scheduled date for the next regulatory surveillance audit or internal quality audit.
Internal short code or identifier for the maintenance organisation used in operational systems.
Legal name of the approved maintenance organisation (AMO) as registered with the issuing authority.
Classification of the maintenance organisation: internal base maintenance, internal line maintenance, third-party MRO provider, OEM service center, component repair shop, or engine overhaul shop.
Postal or ZIP code of the maintenance organisation facility address.
Indicates whether this maintenance organisation is designated as a preferred or strategic vendor for outsourced MRO services.
Internal quality performance rating score for the maintenance organisation, typically on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00, based on defect rates, on-time delivery, and audit results.
Unique identifier for the aircraft component or rotable part record. Primary key.
reference: AMOS Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System

description: Reference to the aircraft on which this component is currently installed, if applicable. Null if component is in shop or store.
reference: AMOS Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System

description: Foreign key linking to maintenance.approved_maintenance_org. Business justification: component.maintenance_organization_code is a denormalized STRING reference to the maintenance organization responsible for the component. Replacing with a proper FK to maintenance.approved_maintenance

Foreign key linking to airport.station. Business justification: Component tracking and rotable part logistics require knowing which station a component is currently located at. Aviation MRO operations depend on this for AOG dispatch, loan/exchange decisions, and i

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Rotable components meeting capitalization thresholds are fixed assets. Links component to asset record for depreciation tracking, NBV reporting, and asset disposal accounting.

Foreign key linking to fleet.engine. Business justification: When a maintenance.component record represents an engine module or LLP, it must reference the fleet.engine asset record for TSN/CSN reconciliation, LLP life tracking, and lease return condition verifi

Foreign key linking to safety.hazard. Business justification: Specific components may be associated with identified hazards (design flaws, known failure modes). Link supports component-level risk tracking, informs procurement decisions, and enables targeted moni

Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: Components on leased aircraft must track lease contract for lease return inventory reconciliation, maintenance reserve claims, and lessor asset tracking. Required for lease return audits.
Original purchase or acquisition cost of the component in the airlines functional currency. Used for asset valuation and depreciation calculations.
Issue date of the current airworthiness release certificate for this component.
Unique reference number of the airworthiness release certificate (EASA Form 1, FAA 8130-3, or equivalent) certifying this component as airworthy and fit for installation.
Type of airworthiness release certificate accompanying this component. EASA Form 1, FAA Form 8130-3, and CAA CRS are the most common authorized release certificates.
ATA iSpec 2200 chapter code classifying the component by aircraft system (e.g., 32 for landing gear, 71 for powerplant, 24 for electrical). Industry-standard taxonomy for aircraft systems.
Manufacturing batch or lot number for batch-tracked components. Used for traceability in case of manufacturing defects or airworthiness directives. Null for serialized-only components.
Detailed textual description of the component including type, function, and key characteristics. Used for identification and maintenance planning.
reference: AMOS Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System

description: Two-letter ATA Spec 2000 condition code indicating the maintenance and airworthiness status of the component (e.g., AR=As Removed, OH=Overhauled, NE=New, RP=Repaired, SV=Serviceable).
Timestamp when this component record was first created in the maintenance system.
reference: AMOS Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System

description: Classification of the components criticality to flight operations and safety. AOG-critical components ground the aircraft if unserviceable, flight-safety components affect airworthiness, operational components affect dispatch reliability.
reference: ATA MSG-3, AMOS Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost and other monetary values associated with this component.

Type of location where the component is currently held. Aircraft indicates installed on aircraft, shop indicates undergoing maintenance, store indicates warehouse inventory, quarantine indicates awaiting inspection, scrap indicates condemned.
reference: AMOS Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System

description: Flight cycles remaining before the component reaches its life limit or next scheduled maintenance event. Null if not applicable or unlimited.
Flight cycles accumulated since the last major overhaul or shop visit. Reset to zero after overhaul. Null if component has never been overhauled.
Date when the component was installed on the current aircraft. Null if component is not currently installed on an aircraft.
Timestamp when this component record was last updated in the maintenance system.

description: Date when the component last underwent major overhaul or shop visit. Null if component has never been overhauled.

description: Total aircraft out-of-service hours from work order start to completion. Used for aircraft utilization analysis and maintenance efficiency metrics. Calculated as the difference between actual start and actual completion timestamps.
Planned labor cost for the work order in the airlines reporting currency, calculated from estimated man-hours and labor rates. Used for budgeting and cost forecasting.
reference: IATA Maintenance Cost Standards, IFRS 16 Lease Accounting

description: Planned labor hours required to complete the work order, based on maintenance program standards or engineering estimates. Used for resource planning and cost estimation.
reference: ATA MSG-3 Maintenance Program Development, IATA Maintenance Cost Standards

description: Planned material and parts cost for the work order in the airlines reporting currency. Includes consumables, rotables, and expendables. Used for budgeting and inventory planning.
reference: IATA Maintenance Cost Standards, ATA Spec 2000 Material Management

description: Date and time when this work order record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

The maintenance program check interval or package this work order belongs to. A-check through D-check represent increasing scope and interval; daily/weekly/transit are operational checks; line maintenance is performed at the gate; base maintenance is performed in hangar; component maintenance is off-wing shop work. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: a_check|b_check|c_check|d_check|daily|weekly|transit|line|base|component — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FAA AC 120-16F Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program, EASA Part-M Maintenance Program

description: Additional free-text notes, remarks, or special instructions related to the work order. May include coordination notes, parts availability updates, or engineering disposition.

The trigger or authority that initiated this work order. AD (Airworthiness Directive) indicates mandatory regulatory action; SB (Service Bulletin) indicates manufacturer recommendation; MEL indicates deferred defect; defect indicates pilot or engineer report; interval indicates scheduled maintenance program; modification indicates design change; inspection indicates regulatory or operational audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ad|sb|mel|defect|interval|modification|inspection — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FAA AD System, EASA AD Process, ICAO Annex 8 Airworthiness Standards

description: Reference to the Part-145 maintenance organisation approval certificate number under which this work was performed. Required for regulatory compliance and airworthiness release.
reference: EASA Part-145 Approved Maintenance Organisation, FAA Part 145 Repair Station

description: Business priority assigned to the work order. Critical indicates AOG (Aircraft on Ground) or safety-critical; high indicates flight-impacting; medium indicates scheduled with tight window; low indicates deferrable; routine indicates standard interval work.
reference: IATA Maintenance Cost Standards, SMS (Safety Management System) Risk Matrix

description: Planned date and time when maintenance work is scheduled to be completed. Used for aircraft return-to-service planning and slot management.
Planned date and time when maintenance work is scheduled to begin. Used for resource planning and aircraft availability forecasting.
Date and time when the certifying engineer signed off the work order and issued the Certificate of Release to Service (CRS). Marks the official completion and airworthiness release.
reference: EASA Part-M Continuing Airworthiness, FAA AC 43-9C Maintenance Records

description: Externally-known business identifier for the work order, typically formatted as WO followed by numeric sequence. Used for cross-system reference and operator communication.
reference: AMOS (Aircraft Maintenance and Engineering System) Work Order Standards, ATA Spec 2000

description: Current lifecycle state of the work order. Open indicates work is scheduled but not started; in-progress indicates active execution; deferred indicates MEL (Minimum Equipment List) deferral or resource constraint; completed indicates work finished pending sign-off; closed indicates final approval and record closure; cancelled indicates work order voided.
reference: IATA Maintenance Cost Task Force Standards, Part-145 Work Order Lifecycle

description: Classification of the work order by maintenance category. Scheduled includes routine checks and intervals; unscheduled covers reactive maintenance; AOG (Aircraft on Ground) indicates critical grounding events; modification covers STC (Supplemental Type Certificate) and design changes; inspection includes regulatory and operational checks; defect rectification addresses reported discrepancies.
reference: EASA Part-145 Maintenance Categories, FAA AC 120-16F Maintenance Programs

Indicates whether this city pair is currently active in the airline's route network. Inactive city pairs are retained for historical analysis and potential future reactivation.

Indicates whether this city pair is eligible for codeshare operations under existing alliance agreements and regulatory approvals.

Indicates whether the city pair is defined as one-way (origin to destination only) or bidirectional (both directions treated as same market for analysis).

Shortest distance between origin and destination airports measured along the surface of the Earth (great circle route), expressed in statute miles. Used for mileage accrual in FFP programs.
Network topology classification indicating whether the city pair connects two hubs, a hub to a spoke, two spokes, or operates as a point-to-point route outside the hub network.
