reference: IATA Resolution 722; ARC Industry Agents Handbook; IFRS 15 (Revenue Recognition)

description: Alphanumeric code (up to 15 characters) identifying the specific fare rule set applied to this coupon, encoding cabin, booking class, seasonality, advance purchase, and restrictions (e.g., YLOWUS, QAPEX14). SSOT for fare rule application and revenue accounting.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; ATPCO Fare Filing Standards

description: Indicates the fare construction method applied to this coupon: NUC=Neutral Unit of Construction (IATA proration), SITI=Sold Inside/Ticketed Inside, SOTO=Sold Outside/Ticketed Outside, SITO=Sold Inside/Ticketed Outside, SOTI=Sold Outside/Ticketed Inside. Governs BSP settlement currency conversion rules.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA Fare Construction Rules; BSP Manual

description: ISO 4217 3-letter currency code in which the fare amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for BSP/ARC settlement and multi-currency revenue reporting.

Calendar date on which the passenger actually flew this coupon segment, as recorded by the departure control system at boarding. Populated when coupon_status is F (Flown). Triggers revenue recognition under IFRS 15.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IFRS 15 (Revenue Recognition); Amadeus Altéa DCS

description: Calendar date on which the e-ticket containing this coupon was issued. Used for revenue period allocation, BSP/ARC reporting cycles, and advance purchase restriction validation.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which this coupon record was most recently modified in the source system. Tracks status changes, exchanges, refunds, and reprotection events for audit and incremental load processing.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Amadeus Altéa PSS; ISO 8601

description: Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the coupon was lifted (scanned/validated) at the boarding gate. Captured by the Amadeus Altéa Departure Control System (DCS). Used for OOOI event correlation and OTP measurement.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Amadeus Altéa DCS; ICAO Annex 6

description: Latest date on which this coupon may be used for travel, as specified by the fare rule. Enforced at check-in and boarding. Null indicates no upper validity restriction (open-dated coupon).
reference: IATA Resolution 722; ATPCO Fare Filing Standards

description: Earliest date on which this coupon may be used for travel, as specified by the fare rule. Enforced at check-in and boarding. Null indicates no lower validity restriction.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; ATPCO Fare Filing Standards

description: Fare amount expressed in IATA Neutral Units of Construction (NUC), the currency-neutral unit used for international fare proration and interline revenue accounting. Converted to local currency using IATA Rate of Exchange (ROE).
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA Fare Construction Rules; BSP Manual

description: IATA 2-character designator of the airline actually operating the flight. Differs from the marketing carrier code in codeshare and interline arrangements. Required for BSP/ARC settlement and DOT reporting.

Amount refunded for this coupon when coupon_status is R (Refunded), net of applicable cancellation penalties. Used for revenue reversal accounting, BSP/ARC refund settlement, and customer refund tracking.
Calendar date on which the refund for this coupon was processed. Populated when coupon_status is R (Refunded). Used for revenue reversal period allocation and BSP/ARC refund cycle reporting.

Identifies the settlement channel through which this coupon will be or has been financially settled: BSP=IATA Billing and Settlement Plan, ARC=Airlines Reporting Corporation (US domestic), DIRECT=direct airline settlement, INTERLINE=interline billing via IATA Clearing House.
reference: IATA BSP Manual; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Indicates whether this coupon point constitutes a stopover (True) or a connection (False) per IATA fare construction rules. A stopover is a deliberate break in journey exceeding 24 hours domestically or 24 hours internationally. Affects fare construction and through-fare applicability.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA Fare Construction Rules

description: Total taxes, fees, and surcharges (YQ/YR fuel surcharge, airport taxes, PFC, government levies) allocated to this coupon segment. Required for BSP/ARC settlement and regulatory tax remittance reporting.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; DOT 14 CFR Part 399; PFC regulations (49 U.S.C. 40117)

description: Total amount for this coupon segment (fare_amount + tax_amount) in the ticket currency. Used as the gross revenue figure for coupon-level BSP/ARC settlement and revenue accounting.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; ARC Industry Agents Handbook; IFRS 15

description: Calendar date on which this coupon was voided. Populated when coupon_status is V (Voided). Voiding is only permitted on the date of issuance per BSP/ARC rules. Used for revenue accounting and settlement reconciliation.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; BSP Manual; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Primary key for ticket_exchange

Foreign key linking to revenue.agency. Business justification: Ticket exchanges processed through travel agencies should reference the agency master. Standard N:1 relationship. No bidirectional conflict.

Foreign key linking to ancillary.ancillary_emd. Business justification: Ticket exchanges generate change-fee EMDs. ticket_exchange.emd_number is a denormalized text reference to the ancillary_emd entity. Replacing it with a proper FK enables exchange audit trails, BSP set

Foreign key linking to revenue.bsp_settlement. Business justification: Ticket exchanges (reissues) processed through BSP channels are included in BSP settlement batches. The settlement_plan_type attribute suggests settlement tracking. This should be a FK to the authorita

Foreign key linking to reservation.e_ticket. Business justification: A ticket exchange references the original e-ticket being reissued. Linking enables complete exchange audit trail, e-ticket lifecycle tracking, and conjunction ticket management required for IATA resol

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: Exchange transactions post to GL for revenue adjustments (fare differences, penalties). Required for accurate revenue recognition and financial reporting.

Foreign key linking to flight.flight_irop_event. Business justification: Involuntary ticket exchanges caused by IROP events (cancellations, diversions, mass delays) must reference the triggering IROP event for DOT/EC261 regulatory reporting, IROP cost attribution, and pass

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare. Business justification: ticket_exchange captures the new fare details after reissue (new_fare_amount, new_fare_basis_code as execution-specific values) but has no FK to the fare master. Adding new_fare_id → revenue.fare.fare

Foreign key linking to inventory.flight_inventory. Business justification: Ticket exchange (reissue) moves a passenger to a new flight, requiring confirmation of seat availability on the new flight_inventory. Role-prefix 'new_' distinguishes the target flight from the origin

Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: ticket_exchange captures voluntary or involuntary ticket modifications (reissues). Each exchange references an original ticket being exchanged. The table has original_ticket_number (STRING) as a busin
Reference to the passenger profile record for whom the ticket exchange was performed. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE linking this transaction to the traveller.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Amadeus Altéa Passenger Profile

description: Foreign key linking to reservation.pnr. Business justification: Ticket exchanges must reference the PNR for exchange history tracking, reissue audit, and passenger itinerary reconciliation. ticket_exchange.pnr_locator is a denormalized PNR reference; a proper FK e

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Involuntary ticket exchanges caused by AOG or technical cancellations must be traced to the triggering maintenance work order for irregular operations cost recovery, insurance claims, and carrier liab

Method by which any additional fare difference or penalty was collected from the passenger. EMD (Electronic Miscellaneous Document) is used for ancillary collections per IATA standards. Waived applies to involuntary exchanges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cash|credit_card|emd|residual_value|waived — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA EMD (Electronic Miscellaneous Document) Standard; Amadeus Altéa EMD

description: Audit timestamp recording when this exchange transaction record was first captured in the Silver layer data product. Conforms to yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this exchange transaction are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the point-of-sale currency at the time of exchange.

Principal business event timestamp recording the exact date and time (with timezone offset) when the ticket exchange transaction was executed in the PSS. Distinct from audit timestamps. Conforms to yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.

Externally-known business identifier for the ticket exchange transaction, typically generated by the Passenger Service System (PSS) or GDS at the time of reissue. Used for cross-system reconciliation and customer-facing reference.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Amadeus Altéa PSS Exchange Reference

description: Current lifecycle status of the ticket exchange transaction. Pending indicates initiated but not finalized; completed indicates successfully processed; voided indicates cancelled before settlement; failed indicates processing error; reversed indicates post-completion reversal.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Amadeus Altéa Exchange Workflow

description: Classification of the reason or nature of the ticket exchange. Voluntary indicates passenger-initiated change; involuntary indicates airline-initiated change; upgrade/downgrade reflect cabin class changes; irop_reprotection covers Irregular Operations (IROP) rebooking; schedule_change covers airline schedule modifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary|involuntary|upgrade|downgrade|irop_reprotection|schedule_change — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Resolution 735 (Involuntary Refunds); DOT 14 CFR Part 250; Sabre AirVision Exchange Type Codes

description: Net monetary difference between the new fare amount and the original fare amount (new_fare_amount minus original_fare_amount). A positive value indicates an additional collection (ADC) owed by the passenger; a negative value indicates a residual credit. Central to revenue impact tracking for ticket reissues.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Sabre AirVision Exchange Pricing; IATA BSP

description: Unique transaction identifier assigned by the Global Distribution System (GDS) — such as Amadeus or Sabre — at the time the exchange was processed. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the PSS and GDS settlement records.
reference: Amadeus Altéa PSS; Sabre AirVision; IATA BSP

description: Airline-defined reason code specifying the cause of an involuntary ticket exchange (e.g., flight cancellation, significant schedule change, equipment downgrade, denied boarding). Populated only when exchange_type is involuntary or irop_reprotection. Required for DOT and EASA regulatory reporting on involuntary rerouting.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250; EASA Regulation EC 261/2004; IATA Resolution 735

description: IATA two-letter or ICAO three-letter airline designator code of the carrier that issued the new ticket. May differ from the operating carrier in codeshare or interline scenarios.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA Airline Designator Code Standard

description: Audit timestamp recording when this exchange transaction record was last modified in the Silver layer data product. Used for incremental load tracking and change detection. Conforms to yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.

IATA cabin class code of the newly issued ticket after exchange. F=First, C=Business, W=Premium Economy, Y=Economy. Used in conjunction with original_cabin_class to classify upgrade or downgrade transactions.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; IATA Cabin Class Codes

description: IATA three-letter airport code of the final destination on the newly issued ticket itinerary after exchange. Used for O&D revenue analysis and network planning.
reference: IATA Airport Code Standard; IATA Resolution 722

description: Gross base fare amount of the newly issued ticket in the transaction currency, excluding taxes and fees. Used to compute the fare difference and assess revenue uplift or dilution from the exchange.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA BSP Reporting Standard

description: IATA fare basis code of the newly issued ticket after exchange. Identifies the fare product, booking class, and applicable conditions governing the replacement itinerary. Used for revenue impact analysis and yield management reporting.
reference: IATA ATPCO Fare Basis Code Standard; Sabre AirVision Fare Basis

description: IATA three-letter airport code of the origin point on the newly issued ticket itinerary after exchange. Used for route-level revenue impact analysis and yield management reporting.
reference: IATA Airport Code Standard; IATA Resolution 722

description: 13-digit IATA e-ticket number of the newly issued ticket resulting from the exchange. Conforms to IATA standard ticket numbering. Used to link the exchange event to the replacement travel document.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA Ticket and Document Numbering Standard

description: Scheduled departure date of the first flight segment on the newly issued ticket itinerary after exchange. Used for demand forecasting, load factor impact assessment, and revenue period reporting.
IATA cabin class code of the original ticket prior to exchange. F=First, C=Business, W=Premium Economy, Y=Economy. Used to identify upgrade/downgrade exchanges and assess revenue impact.
reference: IATA Resolution 728; IATA Cabin Class Codes

description: IATA three-letter airport code of the final destination on the original ticket itinerary prior to exchange. Used for O&D (Origin and Destination) revenue analysis.
reference: IATA Airport Code Standard; IATA Resolution 722

description: Gross base fare amount of the original ticket in the transaction currency, excluding taxes and fees. Used as the baseline for fare difference calculation and revenue accounting reconciliation.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) Reporting Standard

description: IATA fare basis code of the original ticket prior to exchange. Identifies the fare product, booking class, and applicable conditions that governed the original itinerary. Used for fare difference calculation and revenue accounting.
reference: IATA ATPCO Fare Basis Code Standard; Sabre AirVision Fare Basis

description: IATA three-letter airport code of the origin point on the original ticket itinerary prior to exchange. Used for route-level revenue impact analysis and yield management reporting.
reference: IATA Airport Code Standard; IATA Resolution 722

description: Scheduled departure date of the first flight segment on the original ticket itinerary prior to exchange. Used for seasonality analysis, advance purchase tracking, and revenue accounting period alignment.

IATA three-letter Passenger Type Code (PTC) applicable to the exchanged ticket (e.g., ADT=Adult, CHD=Child, INF=Infant, MIL=Military). Determines applicable fare rules, penalty waivers, and pricing logic for the exchange.
reference: IATA Passenger Type Code Standard; ATPCO PTC Reference

description: Change fee or penalty amount charged to the passenger for the voluntary ticket exchange, as defined by the applicable fare rule. Zero for involuntary exchanges or IROP reprotections where penalties are waived per IATA Resolution 735.
reference: IATA Resolution 735; ATPCO Category 16 (Penalties); DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: Distribution channel through which the ticket exchange was processed. GDS (Global Distribution System) covers Amadeus/Sabre/Travelport agency sales; NDC (New Distribution Capability) covers IATA NDC API channels; direct channels cover airline-owned touchpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gds|direct_web|direct_mobile|airport_counter|call_center|ndc|interline — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA NDC (New Distribution Capability) Standard; IATA Resolution 787; Amadeus Altéa

description: Identifies the settlement mechanism through which the exchange transaction will be financially settled. BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) for IATA-member markets; ARC (Airlines Reporting Corporation) for US/Canada markets; direct for airline-direct sales channels.
reference: IATA BSP; ARC Reporting Standard; IATA Resolution 722

description: Net difference in applicable taxes and surcharges between the new ticket and the original ticket. Positive indicates additional tax collection; negative indicates tax credit. Required for accurate BSP settlement and revenue accounting.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA BSP; IATA Tax Code Standard

description: IATA two-letter airline designator code of the validating carrier responsible for the new ticket. The validating carrier is accountable for BSP settlement and revenue recognition on the reissued document.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; IATA BSP; Sabre AirVision

description: Airline-issued waiver code authorizing the exchange under special circumstances (e.g., weather events, IROP, pandemic waivers, elite status benefit). When populated, indicates that standard change fees or fare rules were overridden. Sourced from Amadeus Altéa or Sabre AirVision waiver management.
reference: IATA Resolution 735; DOT 14 CFR Part 250; Amadeus Altéa Waiver Management

description: Categorical reason code explaining why a waiver was applied to the exchange transaction. Used for waiver cost tracking, regulatory compliance reporting, and customer service analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|irop|schedule_change|goodwill|elite_status|pandemic|other — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Resolution 735; DOT Consumer Protection Rules; Internal Waiver Policy

description: Primary key for agency
Reference to the parent or host agency if this agency operates as a branch or sub-agency within a larger agency network. Null for independent agencies.
The date when the agency received its IATA accreditation, enabling it to sell airline tickets through the BSP or ARC systems.
First line of the agency's registered business address, typically containing street number and street name.
Second line of the agency's business address for suite, floor, building, or other supplementary location information.
The full legal registered name of the travel agency as it appears in official business documentation and contracts.
Current operational status of the agency in the airline's distribution network. Active agencies can issue tickets and receive commissions.
Classification of the agency based on its primary business model and customer segment served.
Total ticket sales revenue generated by the agency in the most recent fiscal year, used for performance segmentation and commission tier determination.
Seven-digit numeric identifier assigned by ARC to agencies operating in the United States for ticket settlement and reporting purposes.
Three-letter ISO country code identifying the BSP jurisdiction under which the agency operates and settles transactions.
City or municipality where the agency's registered business office is located.
The standard commission percentage paid to the agency on ticket sales, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%). This is the base rate before any overrides or incentives.
The date when the agency's distribution agreement with the airline became effective, establishing the commercial relationship.
The date when the current agency distribution agreement expires. Null for evergreen or indefinite agreements.
Three-letter ISO country code identifying the country where the agency is registered and operates.
Timestamp when this agency record was first created in the system, capturing the initial data entry event.
Maximum credit exposure or outstanding balance allowed for the agency under direct billing arrangements. Null if not applicable.
Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency in which the agency conducts transactions and receives commission payments.
Primary GDS platform used by the agency for booking and ticketing airline inventory.
Eight-digit numeric code assigned by IATA to identify accredited travel agencies globally. This is the primary business identifier used in BSP settlements and ARC reporting.
Date of the most recent compliance or financial audit conducted on the agency's operations and ticket sales practices.
Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in this agency record, used for change tracking and data synchronization.
Scheduled date for the next periodic business review or contract renewal discussion with the agency.
Free-form text field for capturing additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or historical context about the agency relationship.
Additional commission percentage awarded to high-performing agencies based on volume or strategic partnership agreements. Null if no override applies.
The primary settlement mechanism through which the agency remits ticket sales proceeds to the airline.
Postal or ZIP code for the agency's business address, used for mail delivery and geographic segmentation.
Indicates whether this agency has been designated as a preferred or strategic partner receiving enhanced support, priority service, or special incentives.
Email address of the primary business contact for agency communications, notifications, and support.
Full name of the primary business contact person at the agency for operational and commercial communications.
Primary telephone number for reaching the agency's main business contact, including country and area codes.
Internal airline sales region or territory code to which this agency is assigned for sales management and reporting purposes.
Internal credit and operational risk assessment classification for the agency, used to determine credit terms and monitoring requirements.
State, province, or primary administrative subdivision where the agency is located.
Government-issued tax identification number or business registration number for the agency, used for tax reporting and compliance.
The commercial or doing-business-as (DBA) name under which the agency operates and markets its services to customers.
Unique surrogate identifier for each interline prorate agreement or proration transaction record in the silver layer lakehouse.

Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: When our airline owes a prorate share to a partner carrier, an AP entry is created for settlement. IATA interline billing requires AP tracking for amounts payable to partner airlines. interline_prorat

Foreign key linking to revenue.bsp_settlement. Business justification: Interline prorate settlements are processed through BSP channels. The settlement_period attribute indicates settlement tracking. This should be a FK to the authoritative bsp_settlement record. Standar

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare. Business justification: interline_prorate records define how revenue from multi-carrier itineraries is split, and the proration is calculated based on specific fare constructions. Currently fare_basis_code (STRING) is stored

Foreign key linking to route.interline_agreement. Business justification: Interline prorate calculations must be audited against the signed interline agreement (prorate basis, percentage, settlement terms). IATA revenue accounting standards require traceability from prorate

Foreign key linking to finance.interline_billing. Business justification: Prorate agreements drive interline billing transactions for settlement with partner airlines. Direct operational dependency for interline revenue accounting.

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_leg. Business justification: Interline proration is calculated per operated leg — each carrier's revenue share is based on their specific leg. interline_prorate has route_id and origin/destination but no inventory_leg_id. IATA pr

Foreign key linking to reservation.itinerary_segment. Business justification: Interline proration is calculated per flight segment to allocate revenue between partner carriers. Linking to itinerary_segment enables segment-level interline revenue allocation, dispute resolution,

Foreign key linking to route.carrier. Business justification: Interline prorate records must identify the own carrier (the airline running this system) linked to the authoritative carrier registry for IATA bilateral prorate audits and interline billing reconcili

Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Prorate agreements are route-specific; route determines applicable bilateral ASA, traffic rights category, and revenue split methodology between operating and marketing carriers. Essential for interli

Foreign key linking to revenue.ticket. Business justification: interline_prorate records define how revenue from multi-carrier itineraries is split between airlines. Each proration transaction references one ticket. The table has ticket_number (STRING) as a busin

Externally-known reference number assigned to the interline prorate agreement, used for identification in IATA billing and settlement communications. Corresponds to the Specific Prorate Agreement (SPA) or IATA pro-rate factor agreement identifier. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Current lifecycle state of the interline prorate agreement: ACTIVE (in force), SUSPENDED (temporarily halted), EXPIRED (past effective period), TERMINATED (cancelled before expiry), PENDING (awaiting activation). LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Classification of the interline prorate agreement method: SPA (Specific Prorate Agreement), IATA_PRF (IATA Pro-Rate Factor), MILEAGE (mileage-based proration), FIXED_AMOUNT (fixed monetary split), or PERCENTAGE (percentage-based split). CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference

description: Cabin class to which this prorate agreement applies, restricting the agreement to a specific service level (e.g., FIRST, BUSINESS, PREMIUM_ECONOMY, ECONOMY). Null if the agreement applies to all cabin classes.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference

description: Method used to calculate the revenue proration between interline carriers: PROPORTIONAL (based on segment distance), EQUAL_SPLIT (equal share per carrier), MILEAGE_RATIO (IATA mileage-based), AGREED_FACTOR (bilaterally negotiated factor), FIXED (fixed amount per agreement). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PROPORTIONAL|EQUAL_SPLIT|MILEAGE_RATIO|AGREED_FACTOR|FIXED — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Total fare amount collected from the passenger for the interline itinerary, in the settlement currency. This is the gross base amount from which the prorate split is calculated. MONETARY_TRIPLET — gross base amount.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Timestamp when this interline prorate record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the prorate amount or settlement value is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Applies to FIXED_AMOUNT and PERCENTAGE agreement types.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IATA Resolution 850; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination point of the interline route or market to which this prorate agreement applies.
reference: IATA Airport Codes (IATA Resolution 763); ICAO Doc 7910

description: Indicates whether the prorate agreement applies to one-way (OW), round-trip (RT), or circle-trip (CT) itineraries. Aligns with IATA fare directionality conventions.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; ATPCO Fare Filing Standards

description: Indicates whether the proration transaction or agreement is subject to an interline billing dispute: NONE (no dispute), DISPUTED (partner carrier has raised a dispute), UNDER_REVIEW (dispute under investigation), RESOLVED (dispute settled).
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Billing Dispute Resolution Procedures

description: Date from which the interline prorate agreement becomes binding and applicable to interline revenue splits. EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference

description: Date on which the interline prorate agreement ceases to be binding. Null indicates an open-ended agreement with no defined termination date. EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Two-letter IATA global indicator code defining the geographic routing area applicable to this prorate agreement (e.g., AT for Atlantic, PA for Pacific, AP for Asia-Pacific, EH for Eastern Hemisphere). Constrains the routing scope of the agreement.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference — Global Indicators

description: IATA Traffic Conference area applicable to this prorate agreement: TC1 (Americas), TC2 (Europe, Middle East, Africa), TC3 (Asia-Pacific). Determines the IATA tariff conference jurisdiction governing the agreement.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference — Traffic Conference Areas

description: Timestamp when this interline prorate record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
Ceiling value for the prorate allocation in the agreement currency. Caps the carrier's revenue share to prevent over-recovery on high-yield itineraries under fixed-factor agreements.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Floor value for the prorate allocation in the agreement currency. Ensures the carrier receives at least this amount regardless of the calculated prorate factor or percentage. Protects against under-recovery on low-yield itineraries.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Free-text field for additional remarks, special conditions, or negotiation notes associated with the interline prorate agreement or transaction. Used by revenue management analysts for context not captured in structured fields.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Interline Module

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin point of the interline route or market to which this prorate agreement applies.
reference: IATA Airport Codes (IATA Resolution 763); ICAO Doc 7910

description: Two-character IATA airline designator code of the interline partner carrier participating in the prorate agreement. Identifies the counterparty airline for revenue split purposes.
reference: IATA Resolution 762 — Airline Designator Codes; IATA Interline Traffic Agreement (ITA)

description: Three-letter IATA Passenger Type Code (PTC) indicating the passenger category (e.g., ADT for adult, CHD for child, INF for infant) to which this prorate agreement applies. Null if applicable to all passenger types.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Passenger Type Codes

description: Six-character alphanumeric Passenger Name Record (PNR) locator from the Amadeus Altéa PSS or GDS, linking the proration transaction to the passenger booking record.
reference: IATA Resolution 722; Amadeus Altéa PSS — PNR Standards

description: Fixed monetary amount allocated to this carrier under the prorate agreement, expressed in the agreement currency. Used for FIXED_AMOUNT agreement types where a specific dollar/currency value is agreed rather than a factor or percentage.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP)

description: The underlying basis on which proration is computed: FARE (based on published fare components), MILEAGE (based on IATA TPM/MPM mileage), WEIGHT (cargo weight-based, for cargo interline), or REVENUE (based on actual collected revenue).
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference

description: Numeric prorate factor (ratio) applied to the total fare to determine the revenue share allocated to this carrier's segment of the interline journey. Used for IATA_PRF and MILEAGE agreement types. Value between 0 and 1 representing the carrier's proportional share.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Pro-Rate Factor Methodology

description: Percentage of the total interline fare revenue allocated to this carrier under the prorate agreement. Used for PERCENTAGE agreement types. Value between 0.0000 and 100.0000.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference

description: Net revenue amount allocated to the own carrier after applying the prorate factor, percentage, or fixed amount to the collected fare. This is the carrier's actual revenue share from the interline itinerary. MONETARY_TRIPLET — net total.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA BSP Settlement Standards; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a prorate AGREEMENT (the standing agreement defining split rules) or a TRANSACTION (an actual proration event applied to a settled interline billing).
reference: IATA Resolution 850; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management — Interline Module

description: Total taxes, fees, and surcharges (e.g., PFC, government taxes, fuel surcharges) associated with the interline proration transaction. Excluded from the prorate base fare calculation per IATA standards. MONETARY_TRIPLET — adjustment amount.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; DOT 14 CFR Part 399; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Two-character IATA airline designator code of the carrier that issued the ticket for the interline itinerary. The ticketing carrier may differ from the operating carrier and determines BSP/ARC settlement responsibility.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA BSP Settlement Standards; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Date on which the actual proration transaction was applied to a settled interline billing. Populated only for TRANSACTION record types; null for AGREEMENT records. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for an intermediate connecting point that further qualifies the applicable market for this prorate agreement. Null if the agreement applies to all routings between origin and destination.
reference: IATA Resolution 850; IATA Tariff Coordinating Conference

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the yield management parameter configuration record. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to fleet.aircraft_type. Business justification: Revenue management systems set yield parameters (load factor targets, overbooking authorization rates, dynamic pricing floors) per aircraft type because seat capacity and cabin mix differ by type. An

Foreign key linking to inventory.cabin_class. Business justification: Yield parameters (load_factor_target, overbooking_authorization_rate, dynamic_pricing_ceiling) are always cabin-specific in RM systems. An RM analyst configuring yield parameters for Business vs Econo

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_class. Business justification: yield_parameter is a revenue management configuration record that defines optimization settings at the booking class level. Currently cabin_class (STRING) is stored as a denormalized reference with no

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare_family. Business justification: Yield management parameters can be configured at the fare family level (branded fare optimization). This links yield management settings to the fare family being optimized. Standard N:1 relationship.

Foreign key linking to revenue.fare. Business justification: Yield parameters define revenue management optimization settings (RASK targets, load factor targets, overbooking rates, bid price curves) that are configured FOR specific fare contexts. The yield_para

Foreign key linking to route.route. Business justification: Revenue management teams configure yield parameters (load factor targets, overbooking rates, dynamic pricing floors/ceilings) at the route level. This FK enables route-level yield optimization reports

Foreign key linking to route.schedule_season. Business justification: Yield parameters are season-specific (IATA summer/winter seasons drive distinct demand curves and overbooking rates). Revenue management configures separate parameter sets per season. The existing sea
Name or employee identifier of the Revenue Manager who authorized this yield parameter configuration for production use. Supports audit trail and governance accountability.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Number of booking class availability thresholds (authorization levels) configured for this yield parameter set. Defines how many fare class buckets are actively managed by the RM optimization engine.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Resolution 728

description: Historical booking cancellation rate (0.0000–1.0000) for this market or route, used alongside no-show rate to calibrate overbooking levels and availability controls.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation and Inventory; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

business_glossary_term: Cost per Available Seat Kilometer (CASK) Benchmark
description: Cost per Available Seat Kilometer (CASK) benchmark in the operating currency used as the cost floor reference for yield optimization decisions. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO cost accounting.
reference: IATA Economics; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; DOT Form 41

description: Timestamp when this yield parameter configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary yield parameters (RASK target, CASK benchmark, yield floor, pricing bounds) are expressed for this configuration.
Three-letter IATA airport code for the destination point of the market or route to which this yield parameter applies. Null for global or flight-group-level parameters.

Maximum acceptable revenue dilution rate — the percentage of passengers who could have paid a higher fare but were sold a lower fare class. Constrains how aggressively lower booking classes are opened.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Maximum fare amount (in operating currency) above which the dynamic pricing engine is not permitted to set a fare for this market or route. Prevents consumer protection violations and brand damage.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT Consumer Protection Regulations; IATA Dynamic Pricing Guidelines

description: Indicates whether dynamic (continuous) pricing is enabled for this yield parameter configuration. When true, the RM engine applies real-time fare adjustments within authorized bounds rather than discrete fare class steps.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Dynamic Pricing Guidelines

description: Minimum fare amount (in operating currency) below which the dynamic pricing engine is not permitted to set a fare for this market or route. Protects against revenue dilution under automated pricing.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Dynamic Pricing Guidelines

description: Calendar date from which this yield parameter configuration becomes active and is applied by the RM optimization engine. Aligns with schedule season or pricing period start.
reference: IATA Scheduling Guidelines; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Calendar date on which this yield parameter configuration expires and is no longer applied. Null for open-ended configurations. Aligns with schedule season or pricing period end.
reference: IATA Scheduling Guidelines; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Number of days ahead for which the demand forecast is generated and applied in yield optimization. Defines the planning window for booking class availability controls.
Type of demand forecasting model applied by the RM optimization engine for this parameter set. Determines how historical booking curves and pickup patterns are weighted in availability decisions.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Minimum number of passengers (Pax) that qualifies a booking as a group booking, triggering separate group inventory controls and pricing rules distinct from individual seat availability management.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Resolution 735

description: Weighting factor (0.0000–1.0000) applied to historical booking data in the blended demand forecast model. Higher values favor historical patterns; lower values favor recent pickup trends. Must sum to 1.0 with pickup_weight.
Date on which this yield parameter configuration was most recently reviewed by the Revenue Management team. Supports governance tracking and parameter staleness detection.
Minimum acceptable load factor threshold below which the RM system triggers promotional availability or fare class opening actions. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Target load factor (percentage of available seats filled with revenue passengers) for this market or route configuration. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Balances spoilage risk against dilution risk in RM optimization.
reference: IATA Economics; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Minimum acceptable yield (revenue per Revenue Passenger Kilometer) below which seat inventory must not be sold. Acts as the absolute revenue protection floor in bid price calculations. Expressed in operating currency per RPK.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Historical no-show rate (0.0000–1.0000) for this market or route, used as an input to overbooking authorization calculations. Sourced from Amadeus Altéa PSS departure control data.
reference: Amadeus Altéa Reservation and Inventory; DOT 14 CFR Part 250

description: Indicates whether origin-destination (O&D) network revenue management control is active for this parameter set. When true, the RM engine optimizes across connecting itineraries rather than leg-by-leg.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Optimization algorithm applied by Sabre AirVision for seat inventory allocation under this parameter set. EMSR-A and EMSR-B are Expected Marginal Seat Revenue variants; LP-based and network bid price methods support O&D (origin-destination) optimization.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Three-letter IATA airport code for the origin point of the market or route to which this yield parameter applies. Null for global or flight-group-level parameters.

Authorized overbooking percentage above physical seat capacity for this market or route. Expressed as a percentage above 100% (e.g., 5.00 means 5% above capacity). Governed by DOT consumer protection regulations and airline denied boarding policy.
reference: DOT 14 CFR Part 250; IATA Resolution 735; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this yield parameter configuration within Sabre AirVision RM. Used for cross-system reference and audit trails.
Free-text description providing business context for this yield parameter configuration, including rationale for non-standard settings, market conditions, or special events driving the configuration.
Human-readable name describing the yield parameter configuration, e.g., 'TRANSATLANTIC_PEAK_SUMMER_2025'. Used for display and reporting in revenue management dashboards.

Current lifecycle state of the yield parameter configuration record. Active records are applied by the RM optimization engine; draft records are under review; archived records are retained for historical analysis.
Categorizes the scope at which this yield parameter applies: market-level, route-level, flight-group-level, cabin-level, or global default. Determines the optimization hierarchy in Sabre AirVision.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Weighting factor (0.0000–1.0000) applied to recent booking pickup trend data in the blended demand forecast model. Complements historical_weight; both must sum to 1.0.

business_glossary_term: Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK) Target
description: Target Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK) in the operating currency for this market or route configuration. Expressed as revenue per ASK. Used as the primary revenue performance benchmark in RM optimization.
reference: IATA Economics; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT Form 41

description: Scheduled frequency at which the Revenue Management team reviews and potentially updates this yield parameter configuration. Drives the RM governance and parameter maintenance workflow.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Maximum acceptable seat spoilage rate (percentage of seats departing empty) tolerated before the RM system adjusts availability controls to open lower fare classes. Expressed as a percentage.
reference: Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Revenue Management Guidelines

description: Timestamp when this yield parameter configuration record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management

description: Unique surrogate primary key for each vendor invoice record in the accounts payable ledger. Generated by the Databricks Silver Layer ingestion pipeline from SAP FI-AP document entries.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each AP invoice belongs to ONE company code. The accounts_payable table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SAP FI
Reference to the cost center to which this invoice expense is allocated, supporting CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting and departmental cost accounting within SAP CO.

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: AP invoices can be for the purchase or capitalization of fixed assets (aircraft, engines, ground equipment). A FK from accounts_payable to fixed_asset links the payment obligation to the asset being a

Foreign key linking to fleet.fleet_order. Business justification: Aircraft purchase progress payments and delivery milestone invoices are AP transactions that must reference the originating fleet order for three-way matching, CAPEX payment tracking, and purchase ord

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: AP invoices and payments generate GL postings in SAP FI-AP. Every accounts_payable record corresponds to one or more GL entries. This FK normalizes the relationship between the AP sub-ledger and the g

Foreign key linking to airport.ground_handler. Business justification: AP invoices are issued by ground handlers for ramp, baggage, and passenger handling services. Linking AP to the ground_handler vendor record enables vendor payment tracking, SLA-linked invoice validat

Foreign key linking to finance.lease_contract. Business justification: AP invoices for IFRS 16 lease payments should be linked to the lease contract. The accounts_payable table has ifrs16_lease_flag (BOOLEAN) indicating lease-related invoices, but no explicit FK. Adding

Current status of the invoice within the internal approval workflow, reflecting authorization levels required before payment execution. Supports segregation of duties and internal controls per SOX compliance requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Workflow (SWI); Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404 Internal Controls

description: The IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) or Airlines Reporting Corporation (ARC) settlement reference number for invoices settled through interline or agency billing cycles. Applicable for GDS provider fees, interline billing, and travel agency commission settlements.
reference: IATA BSP Operations Manual; ARC Industry Agents Handbook; IATA Resolution 850

description: The SAP accounting document number of the payment document that cleared (settled) this open invoice item. Populated upon successful payment execution. Corresponds to SAP field AUGBL (Clearing Document). Null for unpaid invoices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG.AUGBL); IATA BSP Reconciliation

description: The timestamp when this accounts payable record was first created in the SAP FI-AP system, representing the initial document entry time. Used for audit trail, SLA monitoring of invoice processing cycle time, and data lineage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF.CPUDT / CPUTM); SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements

description: The cash discount amount available if payment is made by the discount_due_date, as negotiated in the vendor payment terms. Supports working capital optimization and supplier relationship management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Terms (BSEG.SKFBT); IFRS IAS 7

description: The deadline by which early payment must be made to qualify for the cash discount offered under the vendor's payment terms (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Supports cash flow optimization and working capital management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Terms (T052); IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows

description: The calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with agreed payment terms and avoid late payment penalties. Derived from invoice date plus payment term days. Corresponds to SAP field FAEDT (Net Due Date).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (FAEDE.FAEDT); DOT Prompt Payment Act Compliance

description: The exchange rate applied to translate the invoice currency amount to the local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP exchange rate table (TCURR). Critical for FX exposure reporting and treasury operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF.KURSF / TCURR); IFRS IAS 21; IATA Currency Clearance Service (CCS)

description: The fiscal posting period (1–16, including special periods) within the fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded. Supports monthly accrual management and period-end close processes in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF.MONAT); IFRS IAS 34 Interim Financial Reporting

description: The fiscal year in which this vendor invoice is posted in SAP FI. Used for period-end closing, financial reporting, and year-over-year cost analysis. Corresponds to SAP field GJAHR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF.GJAHR); IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: For fuel vendor invoices, the volume of jet fuel (Jet-A or Jet-A1) uplifted in liters as recorded on the fuel receipt. Supports fuel cost per liter analysis, CASK fuel component reporting, and CORSIA carbon emissions accounting. Null for non-fuel invoices.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Management Guidelines; CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation); ICAO Annex 16 Vol IV

description: The SAP general ledger account number to which the invoice expense is posted, enabling cost classification for financial reporting and CASK component analysis (e.g., fuel cost GL, MRO cost GL, airport charges GL). Corresponds to SAP field HKONT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL (SKA1.SAKNR); IFRS IAS 1 Chart of Accounts; IATA Uniform System of Accounts

description: The date on which goods or services were confirmed as received, forming the third leg of the three-way match (PO → Goods Receipt → Invoice). Critical for MRO parts, fuel uplift, and catering deliveries. Corresponds to SAP MIGO goods receipt posting date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Inventory Management (MKPF.BUDAT); IATA Ground Handling Agreement Standards

description: The total gross amount of the vendor invoice before deduction of taxes, withholding tax, or discounts, expressed in the invoice currency. Represents the full obligation as stated on the vendor's invoice document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG.WRBTR); IFRS IAS 37 Provisions and Contingent Liabilities

description: Indicates whether this invoice relates to a lease obligation subject to IFRS 16 Leases accounting treatment (e.g., aircraft dry lease, wet lease, ACMI lease payments, airport gate lease). When true, the payment is classified as a lease liability repayment rather than an operating expense.
reference: IFRS 16 Leases; SAP S/4HANA RE-FX Lease Accounting; IATA IFRS 16 Implementation Guidance

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the vendor invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the company code local currency, requiring foreign exchange translation for GL posting. Corresponds to SAP field WAERS.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF.WAERS); IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The date printed on the vendor's invoice document, representing the business event date for the supply of goods or services. Used as the baseline for payment term calculation and tax point determination. Corresponds to SAP field BLDAT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BKPF.BLDAT); IATA BSP Billing Cycle Standards

description: The externally-assigned invoice number as printed on the vendor's invoice document. Used for vendor reconciliation, dispute resolution, and audit trail. Corresponds to SAP field BELNR (Accounting Document Number) or XBLNR (Reference Document Number).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BKPF.XBLNR); IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the vendor invoice within the SAP FI-AP workflow. 'open' = posted and awaiting payment; 'parked' = saved but not yet posted; 'blocked' = payment block applied; 'in_approval' = pending workflow approval; 'cleared' = fully paid and cleared; 'cancelled' = reversed/cancelled document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Document Status; IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) Rules

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this accounts payable record, capturing changes to approval status, payment block, or clearing information. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Change Documents (CDHDR); SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity

description: The net payable amount translated into the company code's local (functional) currency using the exchange rate at posting date. Used for GL posting, financial reporting, and CASK calculation in the airline's reporting currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BSEG.DMBTR); IFRS IAS 21 Foreign Currency Translation

description: The net amount payable to the vendor after deducting withholding tax and any applicable cash discounts from the gross invoice amount. Represents the actual cash outflow obligation. Calculated as gross_amount minus withholding_tax_amount minus discount_amount.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG.DMBTR); IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows

description: Reason code explaining why a payment block has been applied to this invoice, preventing it from being included in the next payment run. Corresponds to SAP field ZLSPR (Payment Block Key). Null when no block is active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG.ZLSPR); DOT Supplier Payment Compliance

description: The actual date on which payment was executed to the vendor, either via bank transfer, cheque, or BSP/ARC settlement. Populated upon payment run completion. Corresponds to SAP clearing document value date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Run (F110); IATA BSP Settlement Procedures

description: The method by which payment is executed to the vendor. 'bank_transfer' = SWIFT/SEPA wire; 'cheque' = paper cheque; 'bsp_settlement' = IATA Billing and Settlement Plan net settlement; 'arc_settlement' = Airlines Reporting Corporation settlement; 'direct_debit' = automated debit. Corresponds to SAP field ZLSCH.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG.ZLSCH); IATA BSP Rules; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: The SAP payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, 2/10N30) defining the agreed payment schedule, discount percentages, and due date calculation rules for this vendor. Corresponds to SAP field ZTERM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Payment Terms (T052.ZTERM); IATA BSP Payment Terms

description: The date on which the vendor invoice is posted to the SAP general ledger, determining the accounting period for recognition. May differ from invoice date due to processing delays. Corresponds to SAP field BUDAT.
The internal SAP FI accounting document number (BELNR) assigned upon posting of the vendor invoice in the general ledger. Uniquely identifies the FI document within a company code and fiscal year.
The total tax amount (VAT, GST, or applicable local tax) charged on the vendor invoice. Corresponds to SAP field WMWST. Used for input tax recovery and tax reporting to national revenue authorities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Tax (BSET.WMWST); IATA Tax and Fees Handbook; ICAO Doc 8632 Taxation

description: The SAP tax code identifying the applicable tax rate and tax type (input VAT, zero-rated, exempt) for this invoice line. Drives automated tax calculation and input tax recovery reporting. Corresponds to SAP field MWSKZ.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax (BSET.MWSKZ); IATA Tax and Fees Handbook; ICAO Doc 8632

description: Result of the automated three-way match validation comparing the purchase order, goods receipt, and vendor invoice for quantity and price alignment. 'matched' = all three documents agree; 'price_variance' = invoice price differs from PO; 'quantity_variance' = invoice quantity differs from GR; 'pending_gr' = goods receipt not yet confirmed; 'failed' = match failed requiring manual review.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Invoice Verification (MIRO); IATA Ground Handling Agreement Audit Standards

description: Classification of the vendor type supplying goods or services to the airline. Drives cost allocation to CASK components and supports spend analytics by supplier category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mro_supplier|fuel_vendor|airport_authority|gds_provider|catering|ground_handling|bsp_arc — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Standard Schedules Information Manual (SSIM); SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Classification

description: The amount of withholding tax (WHT) to be deducted from the vendor payment and remitted to the tax authority on behalf of the vendor. Applicable for cross-border supplier payments subject to bilateral tax treaties. Corresponds to SAP Extended Withholding Tax (WITH_ITEM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Extended Withholding Tax (WITH_ITEM); OECD Model Tax Convention; ICAO Doc 8632

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each accounts receivable record in the SAP FI-AR module. Primary key for the silver layer lakehouse table.

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each AR document belongs to ONE company code. The accounts_receivable table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. This is a mandatory dimension in SA

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger. Business justification: AR invoices and collections generate GL postings in SAP FI-AR. Every accounts_receivable record corresponds to GL entries. This FK normalizes the AR sub-ledger to general ledger relationship, consiste

Foreign key linking to finance.interline_billing. Business justification: Interline billing settlements generate AR records when partner airlines owe the airline money. An AR record for interline revenue should reference the originating interline_billing record. This FK lin

Ageing classification of the open receivable based on days past due date: CURRENT (not yet due), 1_30_DAYS, 31_60_DAYS, 61_90_DAYS, 91_120_DAYS, OVER_120_DAYS. Drives credit risk assessment and bad debt provisioning under IFRS 9 expected credit loss model.
reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; DOT Financial Reporting Requirements

description: Current lifecycle status of the receivable: OPEN (unpaid), PARTIALLY_PAID (partial payment received), CLEARED (fully paid and matched), DISPUTED (customer dispute raised), WRITTEN_OFF (bad debt write-off applied), IN_DUNNING (active dunning process initiated).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments

description: Value date on which funds were credited to the airline's bank account for this receivable. Distinct from posting date; used for treasury cash flow forecasting and bank reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR Treasury Module; IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows

business_glossary_term: Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) / Airlines Reporting Corporation (ARC) Billing Period
description: IATA BSP or ARC billing period identifier (e.g., 2024-W03 for weekly cycle) to which this agency receivable belongs. Supports settlement reconciliation and dispute management with travel agencies.
reference: IATA BSP Settlement Standards; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Date on which the receivable was fully cleared (matched against incoming payment or credit memo) in SAP. Null for open items. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation.
SAP document number of the payment or credit memo that cleared this receivable. Enables end-to-end payment reconciliation and audit trail from invoice to settlement.
SAP cost center associated with the receivable for internal cost allocation and CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Cost Center Accounting

description: Timestamp when the AR record was first created in the source SAP FI-AR system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports data lineage in the lakehouse silver layer.
SAP credit control area (KKBER) under which the customer's credit limit is managed. Groups customers for credit exposure monitoring across company codes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management Module

description: Approved credit limit assigned to the customer in the credit control area, expressed in the credit control area currency. Used for credit exposure monitoring and order block decisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management Module; IFRS 9

description: SAP debtor account number (KUNNR) identifying the customer — travel agency, corporate account, interline carrier, or cargo shipper — against whom the receivable is raised.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; IATA BSP/ARC Settlement

description: Legal name of the debtor customer as maintained in SAP customer master. Covers travel agencies, corporate accounts, interline carriers, and cargo clients.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; GDPR Article 4

description: Number of calendar days the receivable is past its due date as of the last refresh date. Negative values indicate days until due. Used for DSO reporting and credit risk monitoring.

Standardized code identifying the reason for a customer dispute on this receivable (e.g., pricing discrepancy, duplicate billing, service not rendered, ADM — Agency Debit Memo dispute). Null when no dispute exists. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PRICE_DISCREPANCY|DUPLICATE_BILLING|SERVICE_NOT_RENDERED|ADM_DISPUTE|CREDIT_NOT_APPLIED|INTERLINE_PRORATE_DIFF — promote to reference product]
reference: IATA Resolution 850 (ADM/ACM); SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Dispute Management

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the AR document amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency airline operations and foreign exchange reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: SAP FI-AR accounting document number uniquely identifying the receivable posting within the company code. Corresponds to BELNR field in SAP BSID/BSAD tables.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: SAP document type code classifying the nature of the AR posting: DR (customer invoice), DG (credit memo), DA (customer document), RV (billing document transfer), AB (accounting document). Drives posting logic and account determination.

Date by which payment is contractually due from the customer, calculated from invoice date plus payment terms (e.g., BSP weekly settlement cycle, net 30 corporate terms). Key input for ageing bucket classification and dunning.
reference: IATA BSP Settlement Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: Current dunning level (1–4) assigned to this receivable in SAP dunning program, indicating the escalation stage of the collection notice. Level 0 means no dunning initiated.
Accounting period (1–16 including special periods) within the fiscal year in which the AR document was posted. Supports month-end and period-end financial close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 1

description: Fiscal year in which the AR document was posted, as defined in the SAP fiscal year variant. Used for period-end close and financial consolidation reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 1

description: SAP general ledger account number to which the receivable is posted (e.g., trade receivables, interline receivables, cargo receivables). Drives financial statement classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Module; IFRS IAS 1

description: Total gross amount of the receivable before any discounts, taxes, or adjustments, expressed in the document currency. Represents the face value of the invoice or billing document.
reference: IFRS 15; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Two or three-character IATA airline designator code of the interline partner carrier for interline receivable types. Null for non-interline receivables. Supports interline prorate and settlement reconciliation.
reference: IATA Interline Traffic Agreements; IATA Airline Designator Codes

description: Date on which the invoice or billing document was issued to the customer. Used for revenue recognition, ageing bucket calculation, and payment terms baseline.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: Date on which the most recent dunning notice was issued to the customer for this receivable. Used to track collection activity and enforce dunning intervals.

Timestamp of the most recent update to the AR record in the source SAP FI-AR system, including payment postings, dunning updates, or status changes. Supports incremental data loading in the lakehouse.
Open receivable amount translated to the company code local currency using the SAP exchange rate type at posting date. Required for statutory financial reporting and consolidation.
reference: IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company code local (functional) currency to which document amounts are translated for statutory reporting.
Net amount due from the customer after deducting applicable discounts and adjustments but before tax. Used for revenue recognition and working capital reporting.

Remaining unpaid balance on the receivable at the current point in time, calculated as gross amount minus payments received and credit memos applied. Core field for working capital management and ageing analysis.
reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: Method by which payment was or is expected to be received: BANK_TRANSFER (wire/SWIFT), BSP_SETTLEMENT (IATA Billing and Settlement Plan automated settlement), ARC_SETTLEMENT (Airlines Reporting Corporation settlement), CREDIT_CARD, DIRECT_DEBIT, CHEQUE.
reference: IATA BSP Settlement Standards; ARC Industry Agents Handbook; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: SAP payment terms key (ZTERM) defining the contractual payment conditions, discount periods, and due date calculation rules applicable to this receivable (e.g., BSP weekly, net 30, net 60).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module; IATA BSP Settlement Standards

description: Date on which the AR document was posted to the general ledger in SAP. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the basis for period-end cut-off.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module; IFRS IAS 1

description: SAP profit center to which the receivable revenue is attributed, enabling segment-level P&L reporting by route, cabin class, or business unit within the airline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA Profit Center Accounting; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: Classification of the receivable by originating business channel: BSP_AGENCY (IATA Billing and Settlement Plan agency), ARC_AGENCY (Airlines Reporting Corporation agency), CORPORATE (direct corporate account billing), INTERLINE (interline carrier settlement), CARGO_AWB (Air Waybill cargo collection), ANCILLARY (ancillary service charges such as excess baggage, upgrades).
reference: IATA BSP Settlement Standards; ARC Industry Agents Handbook; IATA Interline Traffic Agreements

description: Status of the receivable reconciliation against the originating billing system (BSP, ARC, cargo system, or ancillary billing): MATCHED (confirmed and reconciled), UNMATCHED (discrepancy identified), DISPUTED (formal dispute raised), PENDING_REVIEW (awaiting finance review).
reference: IATA BSP Settlement Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: External reference number associated with the receivable, such as the BSP billing period reference, ARC settlement report number, Air Waybill (AWB) number for cargo, or PNR for passenger billings. Enables cross-system reconciliation.
reference: IATA BSP Settlement Standards; ARC Industry Agents Handbook; IATA AWB Standards

description: Tax component of the receivable including applicable government taxes, Passenger Facility Charges (PFC), and other statutory levies included in the invoice. Supports tax reporting and reconciliation.
reference: IFRS IAS 12; DOT Passenger Facility Charge Regulations; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: Amount written off as bad debt for this receivable, in document currency. Populated when ar_status is WRITTEN_OFF. Supports bad debt expense reporting and IFRS 9 expected credit loss provisioning.
reference: IFRS 9 Financial Instruments; SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Module

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the cost centre record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the cost_centre data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Cost Centre Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each cost center belongs to ONE company code. The cost_centre table has company_code (STRING) which should be replaced with a FK to the company_code master. Cost centers are assigned to company codes

Primary SAP CO activity type associated with this cost centre, defining the unit of output for internal activity allocation (e.g., 'FLT-HRS' = flight hours, 'BLK-HRS' = block hours, 'MRO-MHR' = maintenance man-hours, 'GND-TRN' = ground turns). Used to calculate internal activity rates for cost allocation between centres.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM Activity-Based Costing; IATA Managerial Accounting Guidelines

description: Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost centre for the current fiscal year, in the controlling area currency. Represents the authorised spending limit for departmental P&L management. Used in budget vs. actual variance reporting and financial planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Budget Management; IATA Financial Planning Guidelines; IAS 1

description: SAP CO planning version identifier for the budget associated with this cost centre (e.g., '000' = actuals, '001' = original budget, '002' = revised forecast, 'PLAN' = annual plan). Enables comparison of actuals vs. budget vs. forecast in variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM Planning Versions; IATA Financial Planning Guidelines

description: SAP FI business area assigned to this cost centre, representing a distinct area of operations for internal balance sheet and P&L reporting (e.g., 'PASS' = Passenger, 'CARG' = Cargo, 'MRO' = Maintenance). Enables segment reporting per IFRS 8 without full profit centre accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Business Area; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

business_glossary_term: Cost per Available Seat Kilometer (CASK) Allocation Key
description: Allocation driver used to apportion this cost centre's costs to Available Seat Kilometers (ASK) for CASK reporting. 'ask' = direct ASK-based allocation; 'block_hours' = by block hours flown; 'departures' = by number of departures; 'pax' = by passenger count; 'revenue' = by revenue share; 'equal' = equal split; 'manual' = manually defined percentages. Critical for unit cost benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ask|block_hours|departures|pax|revenue|equal|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IATA CASK Methodology; IATA Airline Cost Management Group (ACMG)

description: SAP CO cost centre category that classifies the functional nature of the centre (e.g., 'F' = Flight Operations, 'M' = Maintenance, 'G' = Ground Handling, 'A' = Administration, 'C' = Cargo, 'L' = Loyalty). Controls which activity types and statistical key figures can be posted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: F|M|G|A|C|L|S|T — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Category Configuration; IATA Cost Classification

description: Classification of the responsibility centre by financial accountability type. 'cost' centres track expenditure only (e.g., maintenance base); 'profit' centres track both revenue and cost for P&L reporting (e.g., route group, cargo division); 'investment' centres include capital employed; 'revenue' centres track revenue attribution (e.g., lounge, ancillary). Drives CASK/RASK allocation methodology.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IATA Managerial Accounting Guidelines; IFRS 8

description: SAP CO controlling area to which this cost centre belongs. The controlling area defines the organisational unit within which cost accounting is performed (e.g., 'AA01' for the airline group). Determines currency, fiscal year variant, and chart of accounts applicable to this centre.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM Enterprise Structure; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: Indicates whether this cost centre is subject to CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation) emissions monitoring and offset cost accounting. True for international route operations cost centres. Drives inclusion in ICAO CORSIA compliance reporting and carbon offset cost allocation.
reference: ICAO CORSIA; ICAO Annex 16 Volume IV; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost centre within the SAP CO controlling area. Used in journal entries, budget allocations, and management reporting (e.g., 'FLT-OPS-001', 'MRO-BASE-LHR', 'CARGO-HUB-JFK'). Aligns with SAP CO cost centre number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IATA Managerial Accounting Guidelines

description: Long-form narrative description of the cost centre's business purpose, scope of operations, and cost accountability. Provides context for financial analysts and auditors beyond the short name (e.g., 'Responsible for all direct operating costs of widebody long-haul fleet on North Atlantic routes including fuel uplift, crew costs, and ATC charges').
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IATA Managerial Accounting Guidelines

description: Full descriptive name of the cost centre as maintained in SAP CO master data (e.g., 'Flight Operations — North Atlantic Hub', 'Cabin Crew Training — Base London', 'MRO Line Maintenance — Frankfurt'). Used in financial reports and management dashboards.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IATA Managerial Accounting Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost centre in SAP CO. 'active' = open for postings; 'inactive' = temporarily suspended; 'locked' = blocked for new postings (period-end); 'pending_activation' = created but not yet released; 'closed' = permanently deactivated with no further postings permitted.
Timestamp when this cost centre master record was first created in the source SAP CO system. Used for audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with financial record retention requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; ICAO Annex 6; ISO 9001 Quality Management Records

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost centre's controlling currency (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Determines the currency in which budgets are planned, actuals are recorded, and variance reports are produced. May differ from company code currency for international operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IFRS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The fiscal year to which the cost centre's budget and planning data applies (e.g., 2024). Aligns with the SAP CO fiscal year variant configured for the controlling area. Used to version budget records and support multi-year trend analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM Fiscal Year; IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Indicates whether this cost centre is designated for fuel cost accounting and uplift tracking. True for flight operations and route cost centres where jet fuel (Jet-A/A1) costs are directly attributed. Enables fuel cost per ASK (fuel CASK) component reporting and hedging P&L attribution.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Management Guidelines; CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation); IATA CASK Methodology

description: SAP FI/CO functional area classifying the cost centre by business function for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting (e.g., 'PROD' = Production/Operations, 'SELL' = Sales, 'ADMN' = Administration, 'R&D' = Research). Required for IFRS income statement by function of expense.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO Functional Area; IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: SAP FI general ledger account group associated with primary cost postings for this cost centre. Defines the range of GL accounts permissible for postings, ensuring cost classification consistency (e.g., 'OPEX' for operating expenses, 'FUEL' for fuel costs, 'LABOR' for crew and staff costs).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts; IATA Uniform System of Accounts for Airlines

description: Node identifier within the standard cost centre hierarchy (SAP CO standard hierarchy) to which this centre is assigned. Enables roll-up reporting by business segment, division, or region (e.g., 'FLIGHT-OPS', 'MRO-EMEA', 'CARGO-APAC'). Used for group-level CASK reporting and segment P&L consolidation.
