description: Reference to the SAP CO cost centre to which this budget plan is assigned. Enables cost centre-level budget tracking and variance analysis across airline operational divisions.
The date on which the budget plan received final approval from the designated authority (CFO, board, or budget committee). Marks the transition from 'submitted' to 'approved' status in the workflow.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC Workflow; SOX Compliance; Internal Governance Policy

description: Budgeted Available Seat Kilometers (ASK) for the planning period, representing planned capacity output. ASK is the industry-standard capacity metric (seats × kilometers flown). Used as the denominator for CASK and RASK budget calculations.
reference: IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; DOT Form 41; SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

description: High-level financial category of the budget plan. OPEX = Operating Expenditure; CAPEX = Capital Expenditure; REVENUE = Revenue budget; MIXED = combined OPEX and CAPEX plan. Aligns with SAP CO-PA cost element categories.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; IFRS

description: Budgeted amount allocated to capital expenditure (CAPEX) within this plan, including aircraft acquisitions, fleet modifications, and infrastructure investments. Supports IFRS 16 lease liability planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA (Asset Accounting); IFRS 16; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Cost per Available Seat Kilometer (CASK) Budget
description: Budgeted Cost per Available Seat Kilometer (CASK), calculated as total OPEX budget divided by ASK budget. Expressed in currency units per ASK. Primary airline unit cost efficiency metric used in board-level financial planning and investor reporting.
reference: IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; DOT Form 41; SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

description: The organisational level at which this budget plan is consolidated. 'entity' = legal entity; 'division' = business division (e.g., cargo, MRO); 'group' = airline group; 'segment' = IFRS 8 reportable segment. Supports financial consolidation and group reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Consolidation; IFRS 8 (Operating Segments); IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: The SAP CO controlling area code under which this budget plan is managed. The controlling area defines the organisational unit for internal cost accounting and profitability analysis in SAP CO-PA.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling Area); Internal Management Accounting Policy

description: The timestamp when this budget plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; SOX Compliance; ISO 9001

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the budget amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency airline operations and IATA BSP settlement reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IATA BSP; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The start date from which this budget plan is effective and binding for financial control purposes. Aligns with the fiscal period start in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: The end date through which this budget plan remains effective. Null for open-ended multi-year plans. Aligns with fiscal period end in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: The fiscal year to which this budget plan primarily applies (e.g., 2025). Used for period-based financial reporting and alignment with SAP FI fiscal year variants.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; DOT Form 41

description: The frequency at which this rolling forecast budget plan is updated and resubmitted. Applicable only when is_rolling_forecast is True. Supports planning cadence governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; Internal Financial Planning Policy

description: Budgeted amount specifically allocated to jet fuel procurement and uplift costs. Fuel is typically the largest single OPEX line for airlines. Supports fuel cost accounting and hedging strategy alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO/MM; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; DOT Form 41 Schedule P-12

description: Budgeted net interline billing settlement amount payable or receivable through IATA BSP/ARC for interline ticketing agreements with partner carriers. Supports interline prorate and billing reconciliation planning.
reference: IATA BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan); IATA ARC; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether this budget plan is part of a rolling forecast cycle (True) or a fixed annual budget (False). Rolling forecasts are updated periodically (monthly/quarterly) to reflect current business conditions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: Budgeted amount for aircraft and facility lease liabilities recognised under IFRS 16. Covers both operating lease right-of-use asset depreciation and interest on lease liabilities for dry lease and ACMI arrangements.
reference: IFRS 16 (Leases); SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: Budgeted passenger load factor expressed as a percentage (e.g., 82.50 = 82.5%). Load factor is the percentage of available seats filled by revenue passengers. Used in revenue budget planning and yield management alignment.
reference: IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; DOT Form 41

description: The date on which the budget plan was locked to prevent further modifications, marking the start of the execution period. Locked budgets serve as the baseline for variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; Internal Financial Control Policy

description: Free-text field for budget planners and approvers to record assumptions, justifications, revision rationale, or board commentary associated with this budget plan version.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; Internal Financial Planning Policy

description: Budgeted amount allocated to operating expenditure (OPEX) within this plan, including fuel, crew, maintenance, airport charges, and overhead. Key input for CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; DOT Form 41

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the budget plan (e.g., 'FY2025 Annual Operating Budget', 'Q3 2025 Rolling Forecast'). Used for display and reporting purposes.
Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the budget plan, aligned with SAP BPC plan version numbering (e.g., BP-2025-001). Used for cross-system reference and audit trails.

Current lifecycle state of the budget plan within the approval workflow. 'draft' = in preparation; 'submitted' = awaiting review; 'under_review' = being evaluated; 'approved' = board/CFO approved; 'rejected' = returned for revision; 'locked' = frozen for execution; 'archived' = superseded. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|locked|archived — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC Workflow; DOT Financial Reporting

description: Classification of the budget plan by planning methodology. 'annual' = full-year fixed budget; 'rolling_forecast' = continuously updated forecast; 'supplemental' = mid-year adjustment; 'capital' = CAPEX-specific plan; 'zero_based' = zero-based budgeting cycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA / BPC; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: Version identifier of the budget plan, supporting iterative planning cycles (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v2.3', 'RF-Q2'). Enables tracking of revisions and rolling forecast iterations within SAP BPC.
Number of years covered by this budget plan (e.g., 1 for annual, 3 or 5 for multi-year strategic plans). Supports long-range financial planning and IFRS 16 lease liability projections.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; IFRS 16; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK) Budget
description: Budgeted Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer (RASK), calculated as total revenue budget divided by ASK budget. Expressed in currency units per ASK. Key airline revenue productivity metric for strategic planning and investor reporting.
reference: IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; DOT Form 41; SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

description: Budgeted revenue target for the planning period, covering passenger revenue, cargo revenue, ancillary services, and loyalty program income. Supports RASK (Revenue per Available Seat Kilometer) planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA; Sabre AirVision Revenue Management; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines

description: The date on which the budget plan was formally submitted for review and approval. Supports planning cycle governance and deadline tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC Workflow; Internal Governance Policy

description: The gross total budgeted amount for this plan in the plan currency, covering all line items. Represents the top-line financial commitment approved for the planning period. Used for board-level financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PA / BPC; IATA Financial Reporting Guidelines; IFRS

description: The timestamp when this budget plan record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change for audit trail, version control, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA BPC; SOX Compliance; ISO 9001

description: The maximum allowable absolute monetary deviation between budgeted and actual amounts before a variance alert is triggered. Complements the percentage threshold for large-value budget lines where a fixed amount cap is more appropriate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Internal Financial Control Policy; SOX Compliance

description: The maximum allowable percentage deviation between budgeted and actual amounts before a variance alert is triggered for management review. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 = 5%). Supports financial control and exception reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Internal Financial Control Policy; SOX Compliance

description: Unique surrogate key for the company code record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master

description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: A company_code in SAP represents a legal entity for accounting purposes. The legal_entity master is the authoritative source for legal entity data. company_code should FK to legal_entity to establish

Three-digit IATA numeric airline accounting code (also known as the airline prefix) used in e-ticket stock numbering, interline billing, and BSP/ARC settlement transactions. Uniquely identifies the airline in financial settlement systems.
reference: IATA Resolution 762; IATA BSP Manual; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Indicates whether this legal entity participates in the Airlines Reporting Corporation (ARC) settlement system for US domestic ticket sales. Relevant for North American entities and drives ARC settlement accounts receivable processing.
reference: ARC (Airlines Reporting Corporation) Industry Agents Handbook; DOT Reporting Requirements

description: Indicates whether this legal entity is a registered participant in the IATA Billing and Settlement Plan (BSP) for settlement of ticket sales through travel agents. Drives interline billing and accounts receivable settlement workflows.
reference: IATA BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) Manual; IATA Resolution 850

description: Four-character SAP code identifying the chart of accounts assigned to this company code. Defines the general ledger account structure, account numbering, and account descriptions applicable to all financial postings. Corresponds to SAP KTOPL field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts (KTOPL); IATA Airline Accounting Guide

description: Four-character alphanumeric SAP FI company code uniquely identifying the legal entity within the SAP S/4HANA system. Matches the SAP Bukrs field. Used as the primary business key across all FI/CO postings, journal entries, and financial statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code (BUKRS); IATA Airline Accounting Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the company code within SAP FI. Controls whether new financial postings can be made against this entity. 'inactive' blocks new postings; 'in_liquidation' triggers specific closing procedures under IFRS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Status; IFRS 5 Non-current Assets Held for Sale

description: Identifier of the financial consolidation group to which this company code belongs within the group reporting hierarchy. Used for intercompany elimination, minority interest calculations, and preparation of consolidated financial statements under IFRS 10.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting

description: SAP Group Reporting consolidation unit identifier mapped to this company code. Represents the lowest-level reporting unit in the consolidation hierarchy for multi-entity financial roll-up and statutory consolidation.

Four-character SAP CO controlling area code assigned to this company code. Defines the scope of cost accounting, profit center accounting, and internal order management. Enables CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) and RASK reporting by aligning cost objects to revenue data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling Area; IATA CASK Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this legal entity is subject to CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation) obligations. Drives carbon offset liability accounting and regulatory reporting to ICAO.
reference: CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation); ICAO Doc 9501

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which the legal entity is incorporated. Drives statutory reporting requirements, applicable tax law, and national civil aviation authority oversight.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ICAO Doc 9626; FAA/EASA Regulatory Framework

description: Timestamp when this company code record was first created in the Silver Layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage tracking, and compliance with data governance policies.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Four-character SAP code identifying the credit control area governing credit limit management and accounts receivable risk assessment for this company code. Relevant for interline billing settlements and BSP/ARC credit exposure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Management; IATA BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) Rules

description: Date from which this company code became active and eligible for financial postings in SAP FI. Marks the start of the entity's operational financial lifecycle within the group.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master; IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Date on which this company code was deactivated or ceased financial operations. Null for currently active entities. Used to enforce period-end close and prevent postings after entity dissolution or merger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master; IFRS 5; IFRS 3 Business Combinations

description: Classification of the legal entity by its corporate structure. Determines consolidation treatment, intercompany elimination rules, and applicable accounting standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: airline|subsidiary|holding|joint_venture|special_purpose_vehicle|branch — promote to reference product if additional types are required]
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IATA Airline Accounting Guide

description: Two-character SAP code defining the fiscal year structure for this company code, including the number of posting periods, special periods, and whether the fiscal year aligns with the calendar year. Corresponds to SAP PERIV field. Critical for period-end close and CASK reporting cycles.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant (PERIV); IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the parent group's reporting currency used for financial consolidation. Transactions in local currency are translated to group currency for consolidated financial statements.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IAS 21

description: Two-character IATA airline designator code assigned to this legal entity. Used in BSP (Billing and Settlement Plan) settlements, interline billing, ticket issuance, and ARC (Airlines Reporting Corporation) reporting. Links financial entity to operational airline identity.
reference: IATA Resolution 762 Airline Designator Codes; IATA BSP Manual; ARC Industry Agents Handbook

description: Three-letter ICAO airline designator code for this legal entity. Used in flight plan filings, ATC communications, NOTAM references, and regulatory correspondence with national civil aviation authorities.
reference: ICAO Doc 8585 Designators for Aircraft Operating Agencies; ICAO Annex 2

description: Indicates whether this company code is subject to IFRS 16 Leases accounting, requiring recognition of right-of-use assets and lease liabilities on the balance sheet. Applicable to entities with aircraft dry leases, wet leases, or ACMI arrangements.
reference: IFRS 16 Leases; IATA Guidance on IFRS 16 for Airlines

description: Accounting standard under which this legal entity prepares its statutory financial statements. Determines recognition, measurement, and disclosure requirements including IFRS 16 lease liability treatment and CORSIA carbon offset accounting.
reference: IFRS Foundation; FASB US GAAP; IATA Airline Accounting Guide; CORSIA

description: Date on which the legal entity was formally incorporated or registered with the relevant national company registry. Used for entity lifecycle tracking and statutory reporting.
reference: Local Company Registry Requirements; IFRS 3 Business Combinations

description: Indicates whether intercompany transactions posted against this company code are subject to elimination during group financial consolidation. True for entities within the consolidation perimeter; false for associates or joint ventures accounted for under equity method.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this company code record in the Silver Layer. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and audit compliance.
reference: ISO 8601; SAP S/4HANA Change Document; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: Full registered legal name of the entity as recorded with the relevant national company registry or civil aviation authority. Used for statutory reporting, regulatory filings, and intercompany agreements.
reference: ICAO Doc 9626; IATA Resolution 788; Local Company Registry

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the functional/local currency of the company code as defined in SAP FI. All financial transactions are translated to this currency for statutory reporting. Corresponds to SAP WAERS field.
reference: ISO 4217; IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI (WAERS)

description: Air Operator Certificate (AOC) number issued by the national civil aviation authority authorising this legal entity to conduct commercial air transport operations. Links the financial entity to its operational regulatory authorisation.
reference: ICAO Annex 6 Operation of Aircraft; FAA 14 CFR Part 119; EASA Part-OPS

description: Percentage of equity ownership held by the immediate parent entity in this legal entity. Determines consolidation method: full consolidation (>50%), equity method (20-50%), or cost method (<20%) under IFRS 10/IAS 28.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements; IAS 28 Investments in Associates and Joint Ventures

description: Four-character SAP code controlling which fiscal periods are open or closed for posting in this company code. Governs period-end close procedures and prevents backdated postings outside approved windows. Corresponds to SAP BDATV field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period Variant (BDATV); IATA Airline Accounting Guide

description: Full registered office address of the legal entity as recorded with the national company registry. Used for statutory correspondence, regulatory filings, and legal notices.
reference: Local Company Registry Requirements; ICAO Doc 9626

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the registered office address of the legal entity. May differ from country of incorporation for branch entities. Required for statutory filings and regulatory correspondence.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; Local Company Registry Requirements

description: Abbreviated trading or operating name of the legal entity used in internal reports, SAP correspondence, and financial dashboards. Corresponds to SAP BUTXT field.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Master (BUTXT)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this company code record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking.
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture Standards

description: Tax jurisdiction code identifying the applicable tax authority and tax calculation procedure for this company code. Used for VAT/GST determination, withholding tax, and Passenger Facility Charge (PFC) reporting to DOT.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; DOT PFC Reporting; IATA Tax Handbook

description: Official VAT or GST registration number issued by the national tax authority for this legal entity. Required for VAT invoicing, reclaim submissions, and cross-border tax compliance. Sensitive business identifier.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; IATA Tax Handbook; Local Tax Authority Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each fuel cost transaction record in the finance domain silver layer. Primary key for this data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module — Material Document / FI Posting

description: Foreign key linking to finance.accounts_payable. Business justification: Fuel uplifts generate AP invoices from fuel suppliers. fuel_cost_transaction has supplier_invoice_number (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to the AP document. A direct FK to accounts_payable
Reference to the cost centre in SAP S/4HANA CO to which the fuel cost is allocated. Enables departmental cost accountability and CASK fuel component reporting by operating unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Cost Centre Accounting; IATA CASK Reporting Standards

description: Reference to the specific flight leg for which fuel was uplifted. Enables CASK (Cost per Available Seat Kilometer) fuel component attribution at the flight level.
reference: Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops — Flight Record; IATA CASK Reporting Standards

description: Reference to the physical fuel uplift event recorded in flight operations (Jeppesen / NetLine/Ops). Links the financial accounting entry back to the operational fuel uplift record for CASK reconciliation.
reference: Lufthansa Systems NetLine/Ops — Fuel Uplift Event; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Reference to the General Ledger account in SAP S/4HANA FI to which this fuel cost is posted. Supports financial consolidation and regulatory financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Chart of Accounts; IFRS; GAAP

description: Financial accounting period (YYYY-MM) to which this fuel cost is assigned in SAP S/4HANA FI. Determines which monthly P&L period absorbs the cost. May differ from the uplift month due to accruals or late invoices.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Posting Period; IFRS; GAAP

description: Three-letter IATA airport code identifying the station where the fuel uplift occurred (e.g., LHR, JFK, DXB). Used for geographic cost analysis, into-plane agent reconciliation, and fuel price benchmarking by station.
reference: IATA Airport Codes Standard; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Estimated CO2-equivalent carbon emissions in kilograms attributable to this fuel uplift, calculated using ICAO/CORSIA emission factors applied to the fuel quantity and SAF blend. Required for CORSIA offsetting obligations and ESG regulatory reporting.
reference: CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation); ICAO Doc 9501 — Environmental Technical Manual; ISO 14001

description: Timestamp when this fuel cost transaction record was first created in the source system (SAP S/4HANA). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Document Creation Timestamp; ISO 9001 Audit Trail Requirements

description: Reference number of any active or resolved supplier dispute raised against this fuel cost transaction (e.g., quantity discrepancy, pricing error). Populated when transaction_status = 'disputed'. Used for accounts payable dispute management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — Accounts Payable Dispute Management; IATA Fuel Invoice Reconciliation Guidelines

description: Fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this fuel cost transaction belongs in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for annual financial reporting, budget vs. actual variance analysis, and year-end close.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Fiscal Year; IFRS; DOT Financial Reporting Requirements

description: Measured fuel density in kilograms per litre at the time of uplift, as recorded on the fuel delivery receipt. Used to reconcile volumetric (litres) and mass (kg) quantities and to validate fuel quality compliance.
reference: IATA Fuel Quality Requirements; ASTM D1655; ICAO Annex 6

description: Name of the fuel supplier (oil major or independent) who provided the fuel (e.g., Shell Aviation, BP Aviation, ExxonMobil Aviation, World Fuel Services). Used for supplier spend analysis and contract compliance monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Type of aviation fuel uplifted. 'Jet-A1' is the standard international jet fuel; 'SAF' is Sustainable Aviation Fuel; 'Jet-A' is the US domestic equivalent; 'TS-1' is Russian-standard jet fuel; 'JP-8' is military-grade. Critical for CORSIA carbon accounting and SAF blending cost tracking.
reference: IATA Fuel Quality Requirements; CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation); ASTM D1655

description: Net fuel cost translated into the airline's functional (reporting) currency using the exchange rate at the posting date. Required for consolidated P&L reporting and CASK calculation in a single currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Local Currency Amount; IFRS IAS 21 Foreign Currency Transactions

description: Exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the functional currency at the posting date. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA treasury exchange rate tables. Required for IFRS IAS 21 compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Exchange Rate; IFRS IAS 21

description: Total gross cost of the fuel uplift before any discounts, surcharges, or tax adjustments, in the transaction currency. Calculated as quantity × unit price. Base figure for CASK fuel component reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Invoice Amount; IATA CASK Reporting Standards; IFRS

description: Monetary gain or loss on the fuel hedge for this uplift, calculated as (hedged_unit_price - actual unit_price) × quantity. Positive = hedge gain (actual price exceeded hedged price); negative = hedge loss. Posted to the hedge reserve in OCI under IFRS 9.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TRM — Hedge Settlement; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments — Hedge Accounting; IATA Fuel Hedging Reporting

description: The locked-in fuel price per litre from the hedging contract applicable to this uplift. Compared against the actual unit_price to calculate hedge gain/loss and measure hedge effectiveness per IFRS 9.
reference: SAP S/4HANA TRM — Hedge Rate; IFRS 9 Financial Instruments — Hedge Effectiveness

description: Indicates whether the fuel cost is associated with a wet lease or ACMI (Aircraft Crew Maintenance Insurance) arrangement where fuel costs are embedded in the lease liability under IFRS 16. True = IFRS 16 lease-related fuel cost; False = standard fuel purchase.
reference: IFRS 16 Leases; SAP S/4HANA FI — Lease Accounting

description: Name of the into-plane fuelling agent responsible for the physical delivery of fuel to the aircraft at the airport (may differ from the fuel supplier). Into-plane agents are contracted separately and charge a service fee included in the surcharge amount.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA MM — Service Entry Sheet

description: Net total cost of the fuel uplift after applying discounts and adjustments but inclusive of taxes and surcharges, in the transaction currency. This is the amount posted to the General Ledger and used for CASK fuel component reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Net Amount; IATA CASK Reporting Standards; IFRS

description: Free-text notes or comments entered by the fuel accounting team regarding this transaction (e.g., reason for reversal, manual adjustment justification, AOG emergency uplift context). Supports audit trail and exception management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Document Header Text; ISO 9001 Audit Trail Requirements

description: Date and time when the fuel cost was posted to the General Ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for period-end close, accrual management, and financial reporting cut-off.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Posting Date; IFRS; GAAP

description: SAP S/4HANA purchase order number raised for the fuel procurement at this station. Links the financial posting to the procurement process for three-way matching and spend analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Mass of fuel uplifted expressed in kilograms. The operationally authoritative unit for flight planning and weight-and-balance. Used for CASK fuel component calculation (cost per ASK) and fuel hedge effectiveness measurement.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines; Jeppesen Flight Planning System — Fuel Uplift; ICAO Annex 6

description: Volume of fuel uplifted expressed in litres. Standard volumetric unit used for into-plane agent invoicing and fuel supplier billing. Used alongside quantity_kg for density reconciliation.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines; IATA Fuel Quality Requirements

description: Percentage of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) in the total fuel uplift volume (0.00–100.00). Required for CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations, SAF mandate compliance reporting, and ESG/sustainability disclosures.
reference: CORSIA (Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation); IATA SAF Registry; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Total taxes, levies, and into-plane service surcharges applied to the fuel uplift in the transaction currency. Includes Passenger Facility Charges (PFC) where applicable, fuel throughput fees, and local fuel taxes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Tax Line; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines; DOT Fuel Tax Reporting

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the fuel cost transaction is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May differ from the company code currency requiring FX translation for consolidated reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Document Currency; IFRS IAS 21

description: Externally-known business document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI at the time of financial posting (e.g., FI document number or material document number). Used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — FI Document Number; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle state of the fuel cost transaction in the financial system. 'posted' = FI document posted to GL; 'reversed' = reversal document created; 'pending' = awaiting invoice matching; 'disputed' = under supplier dispute; 'cancelled' = voided prior to posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Document Status; IATA Fuel Invoice Reconciliation Guidelines

description: Price per litre (or per metric tonne where applicable) charged by the fuel supplier or into-plane agent for this uplift. Commercially sensitive. Used for fuel price variance analysis and hedge effectiveness measurement.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA MM — Purchase Order Price

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit price is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Fuel is typically priced in USD at international stations; local currency may apply at domestic stations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this fuel cost transaction record. Tracks reversals, price adjustments, and dispute resolutions for audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module — Change Document; ISO 9001 Audit Trail Requirements

description: Method by which fuel was delivered to the aircraft: 'hydrant' = airport hydrant system; 'bowser' = mobile fuel tanker truck; 'pipeline' = direct pipeline connection; 'barge' = waterborne fuel barge (seaplane/amphibious operations). Affects into-plane service fee rates.
reference: IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines; IATA Airport Handling Manual (AHM)

description: Date and time (UTC) when the physical fuel uplift occurred at the airport. This is the principal real-world business event time, distinct from the financial posting timestamp. Critical for matching operational fuel records to financial entries.
reference: Jeppesen Flight Planning System — Fuel Uplift Record; IATA Fuel Cost Accounting Guidelines

description: Primary key for legal_entity
Reference to the immediate parent legal entity in the corporate ownership hierarchy. Null if this is the ultimate parent.
Self-referencing FK on legal_entity (parent_legal_entity_id)
Reference to the top-level parent legal entity in the corporate ownership hierarchy. Null if this is the ultimate parent.
Primary accounting framework used by the entity for financial reporting (IFRS, US GAAP, or local GAAP).
Certificate number issued by the civil aviation authority authorizing the entity to conduct commercial air transport operations.
Date on which the Air Operator Certificate became effective and the entity was authorized to commence operations.
Date on which the Air Operator Certificate expires and must be renewed. Null if certificate has no expiry.
Name of the civil aviation authority that issued the Air Operator Certificate (e.g., FAA, EASA, CAAC).
Accounting method used to consolidate this entity's financial results into group reporting.
Timestamp when this legal entity record was first created in the system.
Classification of the entity's business purpose within the corporate structure (e.g., operating company, holding company, SPV).
Day of month (1-31) representing the end of the entity's fiscal year.
Month number (1-12) representing the end of the entity's fiscal year for financial reporting purposes.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary currency of the economic environment in which the entity operates.
Two-character IATA airline designator code assigned to the legal entity if it operates as an airline.
Three-character ICAO airline designator code assigned to the legal entity if it operates as an airline.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the entity is incorporated.
Date on which the legal entity was officially incorporated or registered with the relevant authority.
State, province, or sub-national jurisdiction within the incorporation country (e.g., Delaware, Ontario).
Timestamp when this legal entity record was most recently updated.
Business-assigned unique code for the legal entity used in financial reporting and consolidation.
Full registered legal name of the entity as it appears in incorporation documents and regulatory filings.
Type of legal structure of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC, partnership, branch).
ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier, a 20-character alphanumeric code for unique identification in financial transactions and regulatory reporting.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where the entity conducts business operations.
Current operational state indicating whether the entity is actively conducting business.
Percentage of equity ownership held by the parent entity (0.00 to 100.00). Used for consolidation calculations.
First line of the official registered address of the legal entity as filed with the corporate registry.
Second line of the official registered address (suite, floor, building name).
City or municipality of the registered address.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the registered address.
Postal or ZIP code of the registered address.
State, province, or region of the registered address.
Official registration number assigned by the corporate registry or companies house in the jurisdiction of incorporation.
Current regulatory standing of the legal entity with the corporate registry or governing authority.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial reporting and consolidation purposes.
Abbreviated or trading name of the legal entity used in operational systems and reports.
Primary tax identification number assigned by the tax authority in the entity's jurisdiction of incorporation.
